translations.source: He observed that milkmaids who previously had caught cowpox did not catch smallpox and showed that a similar inoculation could be used to prevent smallpox in other people. The World Health Organization launched an intensified plan to eradicate smallpox in 1967. Widespread immunization and surveillance were conducted around the world for several years. The last known natural case was in Somalia in 1977. In 1980 WHO declared smallpox eradicated – the only infectious disease to achieve this distinction.
translations.reference: Aliona kuwa wahudumu wa maziwa ambao hapo awali walishika ndui hawakupata ugonjwa wa ndui na alionyesha kuwa chanjo kama hiyo inaweza kutumika kuzuia ugonjwa wa ndui kwa watu wengine. Shirika la Afya Ulimwenguni lilizindua mpango ulioimarishwa wa kutokomeza ugonjwa wa ndui mnamo 1967. Chanjo iliyoenea na ufuatiliaji ulifanyika duniani kote kwa miaka kadhaa. Kisa cha mwisho kilichojulikana kilikuwa Somalia mnamo 1977. Mnamo 1980 WHO ilitangaza kutokomeza ugonjwa wa ndui - ugonjwa pekee wa kuambukiza kufikia tofauti hii.

Kesi ya asili iliyojulikana ya mwisho ilikuwa nchini Somalia mwaka wa 1977. Mwaka wa 1980 WHO ilitangaza ugonjwa wa doti umeondolewa – ugonjwa pekee wa kuambukiza ambao umefikia tofauti hii.

translations.source: This remains among the most notable and profound public health successes in history. Symptoms: Early symptoms of smallpox include high fever, fatigue and severe back pain, and less often, abdominal pain and vomiting. Two to 3 days later the virus produces a characteristic rash with bumps full of a clear liquid, which later fill with pus and finally develop a crust that dries and falls off. The rash begins on the face and hands, then spreads to the rest of the body. Lesions develop in the mucous membranes of the nose and mouth and ulcerate soon after formation.
translations.reference: Hii inasalia kuwa miongoni mwa mafanikio mashuhuri na makubwa ya afya ya umma katika historia. Dalili: Dalili za awali za ndui ni pamoja na homa kali, uchovu na maumivu makali ya mgongo, na mara chache zaidi, maumivu ya tumbo na kutapika. Siku mbili hadi 3 baadaye virusi hutoa upele wenye tabia ya matuta yaliyojaa kioevu wazi, ambayo baadaye hujaa na usaha na hatimaye kuendeleza ukoko ambao hukauka na kumomonyoka. Upele huanza kwenye uso na mikono, kisha huenea kwa mwili wote. Vidonda vinakua kwenye utando wa mucous wa pua na mdomo na vidonda mara baada ya kuundwa.
translations.hypothesis: Hii bado ni mojawapo ya mafanikio makubwa na yenye athari kubwa katika historia ya afya ya umma. Dalili: Dalili za mwanzo za doti ni homa kubwa, uchovu na maumivu makali ya mgongo, na mara chache, maumivu ya tumbo na kutapika. Baada ya siku 2-3, virusi hutoa upele wa tabia na bumps zilizojazwa na kioevu wazi, ambavyo baadaye hujazwa na nanosalio na hatimaye hutoa ukavu ambao hukauka na huanguka. Upele huanza kwenye uso na mikono, kisha unanenea hadi sehemu zingine za mwili. Madoa huendeleza kwenye utando wa mucous wa pua na mdomo na huunda kiroboto hivi karibuni baada ya malezi.

translations.source: Smallpox is transmitted from person to person via infective droplets during close contact with infected people who have symptoms of the disease, or in some cases through contaminated clothing and bedding. It has an incubation period of 7–17 days after exposure and only becomes infectious once a fever develops. People remain infectious until the last scabs fall off. Smallpox was fatal in up to 30% of cases.WHO smallpox recognition card WHO response: The period since eradication has been defined by a lengthy and complex debate focussed on the destruction of the last remaining stocks of live variola virus.
translations.reference: Ndui huambukizwa kutoka kwa mtu hadi kwa mtu kupitia matone ya kuambukiza wakati wa kuwasiliana kwa karibu na watu walioambukizwa ambao wana dalili za ugonjwa huo, au katika visa vingine kupitia nguo zilizochafuliwa na matandiko. Ina kipindi cha incubation cha siku 7-17 baada ya kuambukizwa na huambukiza mara tu homa inapoanza. Watu hubakia kuambukizwa hadi vipele vya mwisho vitoke. Ndui iliua hadi asilimia 30 ya visa.Kadi ya utambuzi wa ndui ya WHO Jibu la WHO: Kipindi tangu kutokomezwa kimefafanuliwa na mjadala mrefu na tata unaolenga uharibifu wa hifadhi ya mwisho iliyobaki ya virusi vya variola hai.

Una kipindi cha ujauzito wa siku 7–17 baada ya kuwasiliana na ugonjwa na huanza kuambukiza tu baada ya homa kutokea. Watu wanaendelea kuambukiza hadi mabomu ya mwisho ya ngozi yatue. Uvimba wa ngozi ulikuwa ukiuwa hadi 30% ya kesi. Kadi ya utambuzi wa ugonjwa wa uvimba wa ngozi wa WHO Jibu la WHO: Muda tangu kuondoa ugonjwa umeelezwa na mjadala mrefu na tata unaozingatia uharibifu wa hisa za mwisho za virusi vya kuambukiza vya variola.

translations.source: In 1996, at the 49th World Health Assembly, Member States decided to have this stock destroyed in June 1999, and only to allow small samples to be kept for research purposes at two designated secure laboratories – one in the United States and one in the Russian Federation. Temporary retention of the existing stocks of variola virus are used for further essential research, which is overseen by the Advisory Committee for Variola Virus Research. This group meets on an annual basis. The WHO Smallpox Secretariat based in WHO Headquarters coordinates all smallpox eradication-related and post-eradication activities. These include overseeing permitted research with live variola virus for the development of countermeasures such as vaccine and antivirals.
translations.reference: Mnamo 1996, katika Mkutano wa 49 wa Afya Ulimwenguni, Nchi Wanachama ziliamua kuharibu hisa mnamo Juni 1999, na kuruhusu tu sampuli ndogo kuhifadhiwa kwa madhumuni ya utafiti katika maabara mbili zilizowekwa salama - moja nchini Merika na moja katika Urusi. Uhifadhi wa muda wa hifadhi zilizopo za virusi vya variola hutumiwa kwa utafiti zaidi muhimu, ambao unasimamiwa na Kamati ya Ushauri ya Utafiti wa Virusi vya Variola. Kikundi hiki hukutana kila mwaka. Sekretarieti ya Ugonjwa wa Ndui ya WHO iliyoko Makao Makuu ya WHO inaratibu shughuli zote zinazohusiana na kutokomeza ndui na baada ya kutokomeza. Hizi ni pamoja na kusimamia utafiti unaoidhinishwa na virusi vya variola hai kwa ajili ya kutengeneza hatua za kupinga kama vile chanjo na dawa za kuzuia virusi.

translations.source: Since the eradication era, safer vaccines and specific treatments have been developed for smallpox and related diseases such as monkeypox. The Secretariat is also responsible for emergency preparedness and manages a smallpox vaccine emergency stockpile in the unlikely event that the disease re-emerges. The Secretariat manages the biosafety and biosecurity inspections of the authorized variola virus repositories as mandated by the World Health Assembly, and ensures annual reporting to WHO Governing Bodies. The Smallpox Secretariat supports WHO Archives in preserving the global history of smallpox and documenting the lessons learned from successful eradication of smallpox. The WHO Smallpox Secretariat's current roles and responsibilities include:
translations.reference: Tangu enzi ya kutokomeza, chanjo salama na matibabu mahususi yametengenezwa kwa ugonjwa wa ndui na magonjwa yanayohusiana kama vile ndui ya nyani. Sekretarieti pia inawajibika kwa maandalizi ya dharura na inasimamia hifadhi ya dharura ya chanjo ya ndui katika tukio lisilowezekana kwamba ugonjwa huo utaibuka tena. Sekretarieti inasimamia ukaguzi wa usalama wa viumbe na usalama wa viumbe hai wa hifadhi zilizoidhinishwa za virusi vya variola kama ilivyoagizwa na Baraza la Afya Ulimwenguni, na kuhakikisha ripoti ya kila mwaka kwa Mashirika Yanayoongoza ya WHO. Sekretarieti ya Ndui inaunga mkono Hifadhi za WHO katika kuhifadhi historia ya kimataifa ya ndui na kuweka kumbukumbu za mafunzo tuliyopata kutokana na kutokomeza kabisa ugonjwa wa ndui. Majukumu ya sasa ya Sekretarieti na wajibu wa Ndui kwa WHO ni pamoja na:

Utawala pia ni mwangalizi wa uandaaji wa dharura na husimamia hisa ya dharura ya chanjo ya doti katika tukio lisitarajiwi kwamba ugonjwa urudi. translations.source: Preserving global smallpox history overseeing the Smallpox Eradication Programme archives in collaboration with WHO Archives and the WHO Library leading on policy discussions and commemorative events regarding smallpox eradication reporting annually to the WHO Executive Board and the World Health Assembly The Secretariat also mobilizes and manages funding for these activities and for ad hoc events as required such as technical consultations and training.
translations.reference: Kuhifadhi historia ya ndui duniani kusimamia kumbukumbu za Mpango wa Kutokomeza Ugonjwa wa Ndui kwa ushirikiano na Shirika la Kumbukumbu la WHO na Maktaba ya WHO kuongoza mijadala ya sera na matukio ya ukumbusho kuhusu kutokomeza ugonjwa wa ndui kuripoti kila mwaka kwa Bodi ya Utendaji ya WHO na Bunge la Afya Ulimwenguni Sekretarieti pia hukusanya na kusimamia ufadhili wa shughuli hizi na kwa matukio ya dharura kama inavyohitajika kama vile mashauriano ya kiufundi na mafunzo.

translations.source: Snakebite envenoming Overview: Snakebite envenoming is a potentially life-threatening disease caused by toxins in the bite of a venomous snake. Envenoming can also be caused by having venom sprayed into the eyes by certain species of snakes that have the ability to spit venom as a defence measure. Inadequate past efforts to control snakebite envenoming has produced fragmented, inaccurate epidemiological data. Many victims do not attend health centres or hospitals and instead rely on traditional treatments.
translations.reference: Kung'atwa na nyoka Muhtasari: Kuumwa na nyoka ni ugonjwa unaoweza kuhatarisha maisha unaosababishwa na sumu wakati wa kuumwa na nyoka mwenye sumu. Uchafuzi unaweza pia kusababishwa na kunyunyiziwa sumu machoni na aina fulani za nyoka ambao wana uwezo wa kutema sumu kama njia ya kujikinga. Juhudi duni za hapo awali za kudhibiti kuumwa kwa nyoka zimetoa data iliyogawanyika, isiyo sahihi ya epidemiolojia. Waathiriwa wengi hawahudhurii vituo vya afya au hospitali na badala yake wanategemea matibabu ya kienyeji.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided English text into Swahili.gs, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the nuances of the language. I've included explanations after the translation to justify some choices.

**Swahili.gs Translation:**

Usumbuaji wa sumu ya nyoka: Usumbuaji wa sumu ya nyoka ni ugonjwa unaoweza kutatiza maisha, unaosababishwa na sumu zinazokuwepo kwenye kinyolezo cha nyoka yenye sumu. Usumbuaji wa sumu pia unaweza kutokea kama matokeo ya sumu inayorushwa kwenye macho na aina fulani za nyoka ambazo zina uwezo wa kurusha sumu kama ulinzi. Majaribio ya awali yaliyofanywa kudhibiti usumbuaje wa sumu ya nyoka yamekuwa hafifu na yamepata taarifa za uchunguzi zisizo sahihi. Wafanywa wengi hawatembei kwenye vituo vya afya au hospitali, badala yake huwategemea wataalamu wa tiba za jadi.

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"Snakebite envenoming"**: I translated this as "Usumbuaji wa sumu ya nyoka."
    *   "Usumbuaji" is a good general term for "envenoming" or "poisoning" in this context. It conveys the process of venom entering the body and causing harm.
    *   "Sumu ya nyoka" means "snake venom."
*   **"Potentially life-threatening disease"**: Translated as "ugonjwa unaoweza kutatiza maisha."
    *   "Ugonjwa" means "disease."
    *   "Unaoweza kutatiza maisha" means "that can threaten life" or "potentially life-threatening."
*   **"Toxins in the bite of a venomous snake"**: Translated as "sumu zinazokuwepo kwenye kinyolezo cha nyoka yenye sumu."
    *   "Zinazokuwepo" means "that exist" or "present."
    *   "Kinyolezo" means "bite."
    *   "Yenye sumu" means "venomous."
*   **"Venom sprayed into the eyes"**: Translated as "sumu inayorushwa kwenye macho."
    *   "Inayorushwa" means "that is sprayed" or "that is thrown."
    *   "Macho" means "eyes."
*   **"Inadequate past efforts"**: Translated as "Majaribio ya awali yaliyofanywa."
    *   "Majaribio" means "efforts" or "attempts."
    *   "Ya awali" means "past" or "initial."
    *   "Yaliyofanywa" means "that were done."
*   **"Fragmented, inaccurate epidemiological data"**: Translated as "taarifa za uchunguzi zisizo sahihi."
    *   "Taarifa" means "information" or "data."
    *   "Za uchunguzi" means "of investigation" or "epidemiological."
    *   "Zisizo sahihi" means "that are not correct" or "inaccurate."
*   **"Many victims do not attend health centres or hospitals"**: Translated as "Wafanywa wengi hawatembei kwenye vituo vya afya au hospitali."
    *   "Wafanywa" means "victims."
    *   "Hawatembei" means "do not go" or "do not attend."
    *   "Vituo vya afya" means "health centres."
    *   "Hospitali" means "hospitals."
*   **"Instead rely on traditional treatments"**: Translated as "badala yake huwategemea wataalamu wa tiba za jadi."
    *   "Badala yake" means "instead."
    *   "Huwategemea" means "they rely on."
    *   "Wataalamu wa tiba za jadi" means "practitioners of traditional medicine."

translations.source: However, available data show 4.5–5.4 million people get bitten by snakes annually. Of this, 1.8–2.7 million develop clinical illness and 81 000 to 138 000 die from complications. High-risk groups include rural agricultural workers, herders, fishermen, hunters, working children, people living in poorly constructed houses and those with limited access to education and healthcare. Morbidity and mortality occur most frequently among young people and children suffer higher case fatality. Furthermore, women experience increased barriers to accessing medical care in some cultures and pregnant women are extremely vulnerable.
translations.reference: Hata hivyo, takwimu zinazopatikana zinaonyesha watu milioni 4.5–5.4 huumwa na nyoka kila mwaka. Kati ya hizi, milioni 1.8-2.7 hupata ugonjwa wa kliniki na 81,000 hadi 138,000 hufa kutokana na matatizo. Vikundi vilivyo katika hatari kubwa ni pamoja na wafanyikazi wa kilimo vijijini, wafugaji, wavuvi, wawindaji, watoto wanaofanya kazi, watu wanaoishi katika nyumba ambazo hazijajengwa vizuri na wale ambao wana fursa ndogo ya kupata elimu na huduma za afya. Maradhi na vifo hutokea mara nyingi zaidi miongoni mwa vijana na watoto hukumbwa na vifo vingi zaidi. Zaidi ya hayo, wanawake hupata vikwazo vilivyoongezeka vya kupata huduma za matibabu katika baadhi ya tamaduni na wanawake wajawazito wako katika hatari kubwa.
translations.hypothesis: Hata hivyo, data zinazopatikana zinaonyesha kuwa watu milioni 4.5–5.4 huugua sumu ya nyoka kila mwaka. Kati ya hayo, milioni 1.8–2.7 huendeleza ugonjwa wa kliniki na watu 81,000 hadi 138,000 hufa kutokana na matatizo. Vikundi vya hatari kubwa ni wafanyakazi wa kilimo mashambani, wachungaji, samaki, wawindaji, watoto wanaofanya kazi, watu wanaoishi nyumba zilizojengwa vibaya na wale wenye upungufu wa upatikanaji wa elimu na huduma za afya. Ugonjwa na vifo hutokea mara nyingi miongoni mwa vijana na watoto hupata vifo vya juu kwa kesi. Zaidi ya hayo, wanawake huona kikwazo kikubwa katika kupata huduma za matibabu katika baadhi ya tamaduni na wanawake wajawazito wako katika hatari kubwa sana.

translations.source: An ongoing crisis restricting access to safe, effective antivenom treatment in many regions, and particularly sub-Saharan Africa, is one factor that contributes to the predisposition for seeking help through traditional medicine. Symptoms: Bites or sprayed venom from venomous snakes can cause a range of acute and serious medical emergencies. Envenoming from different types of snakes can cause different symptoms, some more serious than others. This makes the preparation of correct antivenoms an ongoing problem. Envenoming can cause severe paralysis that may prevent breathing, making immediate medical attention critical.
translations.reference: Mgogoro unaoendelea unaozuia upatikanaji wa matibabu salama na madhubuti ya antivenom katika maeneo mengi, na hasa Afrika Kusini mwa Jangwa la Sahara, ni sababu moja inayochangia uwezekano wa kutafuta msaada kupitia dawa za jadi. Dalili: Kuumwa au kunyunyiziwa sumu kutoka kwa nyoka wenye sumu kunaweza kusababisha aina mbalimbali za dharura za kimatibabu. Ugonjwa wa sumu kutoka kwa aina tofauti za nyoka unaweza kusababisha dalili tofauti, zingine mbaya zaidi kuliko zingine. Hii inafanya utayarishaji wa antivenino sahihi kuwa tatizo linaloendelea. Evenoming inaweza kusababisha kupooza hatari ambayo inaweza kuzuia kupumua, na kufanya matibabu ya haraka muhimu.
translations.hypothesis:

translations.source: People may also experience bleeding disorders that can lead to fatal haemorrhages or irreversible kidney failure. Severe local tissue destruction may also occur, which can lead to permanent disability and even limb amputation. Children are at higher risk of severe effects due to lower body mass than adults. Most deaths and serious consequences from snake bites are entirely preventable by making safe and effective antivenoms more widely available and accessible, particularly in high-risk areas. High quality snake antivenoms are the only effective treatment to prevent or reverse most of the venomous effects of snake bites.
translations.reference: Watu wanaweza pia kupata matatizo ya kutokwa na damu ambayo yanaweza kusababisha kuvuja damu mbaya au kushindwa kwa figo kusikoweza kurekebishwa. Uharibifu mkubwa wa tishu za ndani pia unaweza kutokea, ambayo inaweza kusababisha ulemavu wa kudumu na hata kukatwa kwa kiungo. Watoto wako katika hatari kubwa ya athari kali kutokana na uzito mdogo wa mwili kuliko watu wazima. Vifo vingi na madhara makubwa kutokana na kuumwa na nyoka vinaweza kuzuilika kabisa kwa kufanya antivenom salama na madhubuti zipatikane na kufikiwa zaidi, hasa katika maeneo yenye hatari kubwa. Dawa za kupambana na nyoka za ubora wa juu ndizo matibabu pekee madhubuti ya kuzuia au kubadilisha athari nyingi za sumu za kuumwa na nyoka.
translations.hypothesis: Watu pia wanaweza kupata matatizo ya uchafu wa damu ambayoyaweza kusababisha uchafu mwingi unaoweza kuuwa au kushindwa kwa figo isiyoweza kurekebishwa. Uharibifu mkubwa wa tishu za eneo lolote pia unaweza kutokea, ambayo inaweza kusababisha ulemavu wa kudumu na hata ukatili wa mguu. Watoto wako hatarini zaidi ya athari mbaya kwa sababu ya uzito wa mwili wa chini kuliko watu wazima. Kifo cha watu wengi na matokeo mabaya ya uchafu wa nyoka yanaweza kuzuiwa kabisa kwa kufanya sumu za nyoka salama na bora kupatikana kwa ujanari na kwa urahisi, hasa katika maeneo yenye hatari kubwa. Sumu za nyoka za ubora wa juu ndio tiba pekee yenye ufanisi kuzuia au kurejesha madhara mengi ya sumu ya nyoka.

translations.source: Treatment: Snake antivenoms are effective treatments to prevent or reverse most of the harmful effects of snakebite envenoming and are included in the WHO list of essential medicines. The availability and accessibility of these antivenoms, along with raising awareness on primary prevention methods among communities and health workers, are the best ways to limit serious consequences and deaths from snakebite envenoming. After a bite by a snake suspected of being venomous, follow these steps: Immediately move away from the area where the bite occurred. Remove anything tight from around the bitten part of the body to avoid harm if swelling occurs.
translations.reference: Matibabu: Dawa za kuua nyoka ni matibabu madhubuti ya kuzuia au kubadilisha athari nyingi za kuumwa na nyoka na zimejumuishwa katika orodha ya WHO ya dawa muhimu. Upatikanaji na ufikiwaji wa dawa hizi, pamoja na kuongeza uelewa juu ya mbinu za kimsingi za kuzuia miongoni mwa jamii na wahudumu wa afya, ni njia bora za kupunguza madhara makubwa na vifo kutokana na kuumwa na nyoka. Baada ya kuumwa na nyoka anayeshukiwa kuwa na sumu, fuata hatua hizi: Ondoka mara moja kutoka eneo ambalo kuumwa kulitokea. Ondoa kitu chochote kinachokaza karibu na sehemu ya mwili iliyoumwa ili kuepusha madhara ikiwa uvimbe utatokea.

translations.source: Reassure the victim, as most venomous snake bites do not cause immediate death. Immobilize the person completely and transport the person to a health facility as soon as possible Applying pressure at the bite site with a pressure pad may be suitable in some cases. Avoid traditional first aid methods or herbal medicines. Paracetamol may be given for local pain (which can be severe).
translations.reference: Mhakikishie mhathiriwa, kwani kuumwa na nyoka wengi wenye sumu hakusababishi kifo cha papo hapo. Zuisha mtu huyo kabisa na umpeleke kwenye kituo cha afya haraka iwezekanavyo Kuweka shinikizo kwenye sehemu za kung'atwa na padi ya shinikizo inayoweza kufaa katika baadhi ya matukio. Epuka njia za jadi za misaada ya kwanza au dawa za mitishamba. Paracetamol inaweza kutolewa kwa maumivu ya ndani (ambayo inaweza kuwa kali).
translations.hypothesis: Hakikisha unampa msaada wa kisaikolojia mwathiriwa, kwani mdomo mwingi wa nyoka sumu hazisababishi kifo papo hapo. Punguza haraka mwili wa mtu kabisa na usafirishe mtu huyo kwenda kituo cha afya haraka iwezekanavyo. Kuweka shinikizo mahali palipokuwa na mdomo kwa msaada wa pedi ya shinikizo kunaweza kuwa na faida katika hali fulani. Epuka mbinu za kwanza za kusaidia au dawa za mimea. Paracetamol inaweza kutolewa kwa maumivu ya eneo (ambayo yanaweza kuwa makali).

translations.source: Vomiting may occur, so place the person on their left side in the recovery position. Closely monitor airway and breathing and be ready to resuscitate if necessary

translations.source: Soil-transmitted helminthiases Overview: Soil-transmitted helminth (STH) infections are among the most common infections worldwide and affect the poorest and most deprived communities. They are transmitted by eggs present in human faeces which in turn contaminate soil in areas where sanitation is poor. The main species that infect people are the roundworm (Ascaris lumbricoides), the whipworm (Trichuris trichiura) and the hookworms (Necator americanus and Ancylostoma duodenale). Intestinal worms produce a wide range of symptoms including intestinal manifestations (diarrhoea, abdominal pain), general malaise and weakness.
translations.reference: helminthiases ya udongo Muhtasari: Maambukizi ya ugonjwa wa helminth (STH) ni miongoni mwa maambukizi ya kawaida duniani kote na huathiri jamii maskini zaidi na maskini zaidi. Husambazwa na mayai yaliyopo kwenye kinyesi cha binadamu ambayo nayo huchafua udongo katika maeneo ambayo usafi wa mazingira ni duni. Aina kuu zinazoambukiza watu ni minyoo (Ascaris lumbricoides), mjeledi (Trichuris trichiura) na ndoano (Necator americanus na Ancylostoma duodenale). Minyoo ya matumbo hutoa dalili nyingi ikiwa ni pamoja na udhihirisho wa matumbo (kuhara, maumivu ya tumbo), malaise ya jumla na udhaifu.

Yanatokea kutokana na mayai yaliyomo kwenye kinyesi cha binadamu ambacho kinachanganya udongo katika maeneo ambapo usafi ni mbaya.  Minyoo makuu yanayoambukiza watu ni minyoo mwele (Ascaris lumbricoides), minyoo ya nyundo (Trichuris trichiura) na minyoo ya hook (Necator americanus na Ancylostoma duodenale).  Minyoo wa matumbo husababisha dalili mbalimbali ikiwa ni pamoja na matatizo ya matumbo (diarrhoea, maumivu ya tumbo), udhalili na udhaifu.

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **STH:** I've kept "STH" in parentheses after the full Swahili translation of "Soil-transmitted helminthiases" (Magonjwa ya minyoo yanayoambukiza kupitia udongo) to aid understanding, especially for those familiar with the English abbreviation.  This is common practice in low-resource language translation where acronyms are often introduced alongside the full term.

*   **"Matatizo ya matumbo"**: This translates to "problems of the intestines" and is a clear way to convey "intestinal manifestations."
*   **"Udhalili"**: This translates to "malaise" or "general discomfort."
*   **"Udhaifu"**: This translates to "weakness."
*   **Diarrhoea, maumivu ya tumbo**: I've kept "diarrhoea" as is, as it's often used directly in Swahili, especially in medical contexts. "Maumivu ya tumbo" is a direct translation of "abdominal pain."

translations.source: Hookworms cause chronic intestinal blood loss that result in anaemia. Soil-transmitted helminths are transmitted by eggs that are passed in the faeces of infected people. Adult worms live in the intestine where they produce thousands of eggs each day. In areas that lack adequate sanitation, these eggs contaminate the soil. This can happen in several ways: eggs that are attached to vegetables are ingested when the vegetables are not carefully cooked, washed or peeled;
translations.reference: Hookworms husababisha kupoteza damu kwa muda mrefu kwa utumbo na kusababisha anemia. Helminths ya udongo hupitishwa kwa mayai ambayo hupitia kwenye kinyesi cha watu walioambukizwa. Minyoo ya watu wazima huishi kwenye utumbo ambapo hutoa maelfu ya mayai kila siku. Katika maeneo ambayo hayana usafi wa kutosha, mayai haya huchafua udongo.: mayai ambayo yameshikanishwa na mboga humezwa wakati mboga haijapikwa kwa uangalifu, kuosha au kuchambuliwa;

translations.source: eggs are ingested from contaminated water sources; eggs are ingested by children who play in the contaminated soil and then put their hands in their mouths without washing them. Symptoms: Morbidity is related to the number of worms harboured. People with infections of light intensity (few worms) usually do not suffer from the infection. Heavier infections can cause a range of symptoms including intestinal manifestations (diarrhoea and abdominal pain), malnutrition, general malaise and weakness, and impaired growth and physical development.
translations.reference: mayai humezwa kutoka kwa vyanzo vya maji vilivyochafuliwa; mayai humezwa na watoto wanaocheza kwenye udongo uliochafuliwa na kisha kuweka mikono midomoni mwao bila kunawa. Dalili: Ugonjwa unahusiana na idadi ya minyoo waliohifadhiwa. Watu walio na maambukizi ya nguvu ya mwanga (minyoo wachache) kwa kawaida hawateseki kutokana na maambukizi. Maambukizi mazito zaidi yanaweza kusababisha dalili mbalimbali ikiwa ni pamoja na udhihirisho wa matumbo (kuhara na maumivu ya tumbo), utapiamlo, malaise ya jumla na udhaifu, na kuharibika kwa ukuaji na ukuaji wa mwili.

translations.source: Infections of very high intensity can cause intestinal obstruction that should be treated surgically. S. stercoralis may cause dermatological and gastro-intestinal morbidity and is also known to be associated with chronic malnutrition in children. In case of reduced host immunity, the parasite can cause the hyperinfection/dissemination syndrome that is invariably fatal if not promptly and properly cured and is often fatal despite the treatment. In 2001, delegates at the World Health Assembly unanimously endorsed a resolution (WHA54.19) urging endemic countries to start seriously tackling worms, specifically schistosomiasis and soil-transmitted helminths. The strategy for control of soil-transmitted helminth infections is to control morbidity through the periodic treatment of at-risk people living in endemic areas.
translations.reference: Maambukizi ya nguvu ya juu sana yanaweza kusababisha kizuizi cha matumbo ambacho kinapaswa kutibiwa kwa upasuaji. S. stercoralis inaweza kusababisha ugonjwa wa ngozi na utumbo na pia inajulikana kuhusishwa na utapiamlo sugu kwa watoto. Katika kesi ya kupungua kwa kinga ya mwenyeji, vimelea vinaweza kusababisha ugonjwa wa hyperinfection/usambazaji ambao ni hatari kila wakati ikiwa haujaponywa mara moja na ipasavyo na mara nyingi husababisha kifo licha ya matibabu. Mnamo mwaka wa 2001, wajumbe katika Baraza la Afya Ulimwenguni waliidhinisha kwa kauli moja azimio (WHA54.19) likizitaka nchi zilizo na ugonjwa huo kuanza kukabiliana na minyoo kwa umakini, haswa kichocho na helminths ya udongo. Mkakati wa udhibiti wa maambukizo ya helminth yanayopitishwa kwenye udongo ni kudhibiti maradhi kupitia matibabu ya mara kwa mara ya watu walio hatarini wanaoishi katika maeneo yenye ugonjwa.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided English document into Swahili.gs, aiming for accuracy and clarity while considering the nuances of the language. I've included explanations of some choices after the translation to justify certain phrasing.

**Swahili.gs Translation:**

Maambukizi yenye ukali mkuu sana yanaweza kusababisha uvimbaniko wa matumbo, ambavyo vinapaswa kutendwa upasuaji. *S. stercoralis* inaweza kusababisha ugonjwa wa ngozi na matumbo, na pia inajulikana kuashiria uhusiano na utapiamu wa muda mrefu kwa watoto.  Katika kesi ya kinga ya mwili iliyopunguzwa, mchanga huu unaweza kusababisha ugonjwa wa mwangamizi/usambazaji, ambao ni hatari kabisa ikiwa hautopona haraka na vizuri, na mara nyingi huisha kabisa licha ya matibabu. Mwaka 2001, wajumbe wa Mkutano wa Afya Duniani walipitisha kwa sauti moja azimisho (WHA54.19) likiwaomba nchi zenye ugonjwa huu kufanya kazi kwa bidii dhidi ya minyoo, hasa schistosomiasis na minyoo inayosambazwa na udongo. Mkakati wa kudhibiti maambukizi ya minyoo inayosambazwa na udongo ni kudhibiti ugonjwa kwa kutibu mara kwa mara watu waliokuwa hatarini wanaishi katika maeneo yenye ugonjwa huu.

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   ***S. stercoralis***:  Scientific names are generally kept in their original form in Swahili.gs, especially in medical contexts.
*   **"ukali mkuu sana"**:  This translates to "very high intensity."  I chose this phrasing to convey the strength of the infection.
*   **"uvimbaniko wa matumbo"**: This is the standard Swahili term for "intestinal obstruction."
*   **"ugonjwa wa ngozi na matumbo"**: This translates to "skin and intestinal disease" and is a common way to express dermatological and gastro-intestinal morbidity.
*   **"utapiamu wa muda mrefu"**: This translates to "long-term malnutrition."
*   **"mchanga huu"**: This translates to "this parasite."
*   **"ugonjwa wa mwangamizi/usambazaji"**: This translates to "hyperinfection/dissemination syndrome."  I've kept both terms to reflect the original meaning.
*   **"hatari kabisa"**: This translates to "absolutely fatal."
*   **"kufanya kazi kwa bidii dhidi ya minyoo"**: This translates to "work hard against worms."
*   **"maeneo yenye ugonjwa huu"**: This translates to "areas with this disease" and is used to refer to endemic areas.
*   **"wanaishi katika maeneo yenye ugonjwa huu"**: This translates to "living in areas with this disease."
*   **"mara kwa mara"**: This translates to "periodically."
*   **"watu waliokuwa hatarini"**: This translates to "people at risk."

**Important Notes for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  Swahili.gs, like many low-resource languages, can have multiple ways to express the same concept. The best translation depends heavily on the specific context and intended audience.
*   **Terminology:** Medical terminology can be particularly challenging. It's crucial to consult with Swahili-speaking medical professionals to ensure accuracy and appropriate usage.
*   **Review and Feedback:**  It's *essential* to have a native Swahili speaker (ideally with medical knowledge) review the translation for accuracy, fluency, and cultural appropriateness.

translations.source: People at risk are: preschool children school-age children women of reproductive age (including pregnant women in the second and third trimesters and breastfeeding women) adults in certain high-risk occupations such as tea-pickers or miners.
translations.reference: Watu walio katika hatari ni: watoto wa shule ya mapema/ chekechea watoto wa umri wa shule wanawake wa umri wa uzazi (ikiwa ni pamoja na wanawake wajawazito katika trimester ya pili na ya tatu na wanawake wanaonyonyesha); watu wazima katika kazi fulani hatarishi kama vile wachumaji chai au wachimbaji.
translations.hypothesis: Watu waliokuwa hatarini ni: watoto wa umri wa kabla ya shule, watoto wa umri wa shule, wanawake wenye uwezo wa kuzaa (pamoja na wanawake wajawazito katika miezi ya pili na ya tatu na wajasiri wanaonyonyesha) na watu wazima katika kazi fulani za hatari kama vile wachungaji wa chai au madini.

translations.source: TreatmentThe WHO recommended medicines –albendazole (400 mg) and mebendazole (500 mg) – are effective, inexpensive and easy to administer by non-medical personnel (e.g. teachers). They have been through extensive safety testing and have been used in millions of people with few and minor side-effects. Both albendazole and mebendazole are donated to national ministries of health through WHO in all endemic countries for the treatment of all children of school age. Ivermectin for the control of S. stercoralis is expected to be available at affordable price from 2021.
translations.reference: Matibabu Dawa zinazopendekezwa na WHO -albendazole (400 mg) na mebendazole (miligramu 500) - ni nzuri, bei nafuu na ni rahisi kusimamiwa na wafanyikazi wasio wa matibabu (k.m. walimu). Zimepitia majaribio ya kina ya usalama na zimetumika kwa mamilioni ya watu wenye athari chache na ndogo. Albendazole na mebendazole zote mbili zimetolewa kwa wizara za afya za kitaifa kupitia WHO katika nchi zote zilizo na ugonjwa huo kwa matibabu ya watoto wote wa umri wa kwenda shule. Ivermectin kwa udhibiti wa S. stercoralis inatarajiwa kupatikana kwa bei nafuu kuanzia 2021.

Here's the Swahili.gs translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy suitable for a public health context:

**Matibabu**

Dawa zilizopendekezwa na WHO – albendazole (400 mg) na mebendazole (500 mg) – ni bora, za bei nafuu, na zinaweza kutolewa kwa urahisi na watu wasio wataalamu wa matibabu (k.v.a. walimu). Zimefanyiwa majaribio ya usalama kwa kina na zimetumika kwa milioni za watu wenye madhara machache na madogo. Albendazole na mebendazole zote zinatoa kwa wizara za afya za kitaifa kupitia WHO katika mataifa yote yenye ugonjwa huu kwa matibabu ya watoto wote wa umri wa shule. Ivermectin kwa udhibiti wa S. stercoralis inatarajiwa kupatikana kwa bei nafuu kuanzia mwaka 2021.

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"Matibabu"** - This is the standard Swahili word for "Treatment."

translations.source: Stillbirth Overview: A baby who dies after 28 weeks of pregnancy, but before or during birth, is classified as a stillbirth.There are nearly 2 million stillbirths every year – one every 16 seconds. Over 40% of all stillbirths occur during labour – a loss that could be avoided with improved quality and respectful care during childbirth including routine monitoring and timely access to emergency obstetric care when required. Experiencing a stillbirth during pregnancy or childbirth is a tragedy insufficiently addressed in global agendas, policies and funded programmes. There are psychological costs, especially to women and their families, such as maternal depression, financial consequences and economic repercussions, as well as stigma and taboo.In 2014, the World Health Assembly endorsed the Every Newborn Action Plan (ENAP), which includes a global target of 12 or fewer third trimester (late) stillbirths per 1,000 total births in every country by 2030.
translations.reference: watoto wanazaliwa wakiwa wamekufa Muhtasari: Mtoto anayefariki dunia baada ya wiki 28 za ujauzito, lakini kabla au wakati wa kuzaliwa, huainishwa kuwa mfu. Kuna karibu watoto milioni 2 wanaozaliwa kila mwaka - mmoja kila baada ya sekunde 16. Zaidi ya 40% ya watoto wote wanao kufa kabla ya kuzaliwa hutokea wakati wa leba - hasara ambayo inaweza kuepukwa kwa kuboreshwa kwa huduma bora na yenye heshima wakati wa kujifungua ikiwa ni pamoja na ufuatiliaji wa kawaida na upatikanaji wa huduma ya dharura ya uzazi inapohitajika. Kupata mtoto aliyekufa wakati wa ujauzito au kuzaa ni janga ambalo halijashughulikiwa vya kutosha katika ajenda za kimataifa, sera na programu zinazofadhiliwa. Kuna gharama za kisaikolojia, hasa kwa wanawake na familia zao, kama vile unyogovu wa uzazi, athari za kifedha na athari za kiuchumi, pamoja na unyanyapaa na mwiko. Mnamo mwaka wa 2014, Baraza la Afya Ulimwenguni liliidhinisha Mpango wa Utekelezaji wa Kila Mtoto Wachanga (ENAP), ambao unajumuisha lengo la kimataifa la watoto 12 au pungufu wa trimester ya tatu (marehemu) wanaojifungua kwa jumla ya watoto 1,000 wanaozaliwa katika kila nchi ifikapo 2030.
translations.hypothesis:

**Swahili.gs Translation:**

Ufikeaji wa Kifo cha Mtoto Kabla ya Kuzaliwa: Mtoto anayeanza kufa baada ya wiki 28 za mimba, lakini kabla au wakati wa kuzaliwa, anatajwa kama kifo cha mtoto kabla ya kuzaliwa. Kuna takriban milioni mbili za vifo vya watoto kabla ya kuzaliwa kila mwaka – kifo kimoja kila sekunde 16. Zaidi ya 40% ya vifo vyote vya watoto kabla ya kuzaliwa hutokea wakati wa kujifungua – hasara ambayo inaweza kuepukwa kwa ufuatiliaji bora na utunzaji unaheshimu wakati wa kujifungua, ikiwa ni pamoja na ufuatiliaji wa kawaida na upataji wa haraka wa huduma za uzazi dharura wakati unahitajika. Kupata kifo cha mtoto kabla ya kuzaliwa wakati wa mimba au kujifungua ni jambo la kusikitisha ambalo halijadiliwa vya kutosha katika mipango, sera na programu zinazofadhiliwa kimataifa. Kuna gharama za kisaikolojia, haswa kwa wanawake na familia zao, kama vile unyogovu wa uzazi, athari za kifedha na athari za kiuchumi, pamoja na unyago na tabu. Mwaka wa 2014, Mkutano Mkuu wa Afya wa Ulimwengu uliidhinisha Mpango wa Hatua za Kila Mtoto Mzaliwa (ENAP), ambao una lengo la kimataifa la vifo vya mtoto kabla ya kuzaliwa 12 au chini ya 1,000 kwa kila 1,000 mimba zilizokamilika ifikapo mwaka 2030.

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"Stillbirth Overview" - "Ufikeaji wa Kifo cha Mtoto Kabla ya Kuzaliwa":**  "Ufikeaji" is used to convey "Overview" in the sense of a presentation or introduction to the topic. "Kifo cha Mtoto Kabla ya Kuzaliwa" is the standard Swahili term for stillbirth.
*   **"28 weeks of pregnancy" - "wiki 28 za mimba":**  Direct translation, clear and understandable.
*   **"Nearly 2 million" - "Takriban milioni mbili":** "Takriban" means approximately.
*   **"Every 16 seconds" - "kila sekunde 16":** Direct translation.
*   **"During labour" - "wakati wa kujifungua":**  "Kujifungua" is the standard term for childbirth/labor.
*   **"Improved quality and respectful care" - "Ufuatiliaji bora na utunzaji unaheshimu":**  "Ufuatiliaji" means monitoring/follow-up. "Unaheshimu" means respectful.
*   **"Emergency obstetric care" - "huduma za uzazi dharura":** Standard terminology.
*   **"Global agendas, policies and funded programmes" - "mipango, sera na programu zinazofadhiliwa kimataifa":**  Direct translation, maintaining the structure of the original.
*   **"Psychological costs" - "Gharama za kisaikolojia":** "Kisaikolojia" relates to psychology.
*   **"Maternal depression" - "unyogovu wa uzazi":** Standard term.
*   **"Financial consequences and economic repercussions" - "athari za kifedha na athari za kiuchumi":**  "Athari" means consequences/impact. "Kifedha" relates to finance, and "kiuchumi" relates to economics.
*   **"Stigma and taboo" - "unyago na tabu":**  These are direct translations of the concepts.
*   **"World Health Assembly" - "Mkutano Mkuu wa Afya wa Ulimwengu":** Standard translation.
*   **"Every Newborn Action Plan (ENAP)" - "Mpango wa Hatua za Kila Mtoto Mzaliwa (ENAP)":**  Keeping the acronym in parentheses.
*   **"Third trimester (late) stillbirths" - "vifo vya mtoto kabla ya kuzaliwa vya mwezi wa mwisho (marehemu)":** "Mwezi wa mwisho" means the last month. "Marehemu" means late.
*   **"Per 1,000 total births" - "kwa kila 1,000 mimba zilizokamilika":** "Mimba zilizokamilika" means completed pregnancies/births.

**Important Notes:**

*   **Swahili.gs vs. Standard Swahili:** Swahili.gs is a standardized form of Swahili, aiming for consistency and clarity. I've tried to adhere to these principles.

translations.source: In 2021, 139 mainly high-income and upper middle-income countries had met this target, but 56 countries will not each the ENAP target by 2030 if further efforts are not made. If current trends continue, 15.9 million babies will be stillborn; nearly half of these (7.7 million, 48%) will occur in sub-Saharan Africa. Causes: With quality health care throughout pregnancy and childbirth, most stillbirths are preventable. The major causes of stillbirth include pregnancy and childbirth-related complications, prolonged pregnancy, maternal infections such as malaria, syphilis and HIV, maternal conditions especially hypertension, and diabetes, and fetal growth restriction (when an unborn baby is unable to achieve its growth potential and therefore smaller than it should be). Congenital abnormalities are responsible for less than 10% of stillbirths reported nationally in high-income countries and the exact proportion in low-income settings is unknown due to limitations in diagnostics.
translations.reference: Mnamo 2021, nchi 139 hasa zenye kipato cha juu na za juu za kipato cha kati zilifikia lengo hili, lakini nchi 56 hazitafikia lengo la ENAP kufikia 2030 ikiwa juhudi zaidi hazitafanywa. Ikiwa mwelekeo wa sasa utaendelea, watoto milioni 15.9 watazaliwa wamekufa; karibu nusu ya hizi (milioni 7.7, 48%) zitatokea katika Afrika Kusini mwa Jangwa la Sahara. Sababu: Kukiwa na huduma bora za afya wakati wote wa ujauzito na kujifungua, watoto wengi wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa wanaweza kuzuilika. Sababu kuu za uzazi ni pamoja na matatizo ya ujauzito na uzazi, ujauzito wa muda mrefu, maambukizi ya uzazi kama vile malaria, kaswende na VVU, hali ya uzazi hasa shinikizo la damu, kisukari, na kizuizi cha ukuaji wa fetasi (wakati mtoto ambaye hajazaliwa hawezi kufikia uwezo wake wa ukuaji na hivyo kuwa mdogo kuliko inavyopaswa kuwa). Matatizo ya kuzaliwa nayo yanasababisha chini ya 10% ya watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamefariki walioripotiwa kitaifa katika nchi zenye mapato ya juu na idadi kamili katika mazingira ya kipato cha chini haijulikani kwa sababu ya mapungufu katika uchunguzi.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided English document into Swahili.gs, aiming for accuracy and clarity while considering the nuances of the language. I've included explanations of some choices after the translation to justify certain phrasing.

**Swahili.gs Translation:**

Mwaka 2021, nchi 139, hasa zilizo na kipato cha juu na za kati-juu, zilifikia lengo hili, lakini nchi 56 hazitafikia lengo la ENAP ifikapo mwaka 2030, isipokuwa juhudi za ziada zifanywe. Ikiwa mwelekeo wa sasa utaendelea, watoto milioni 15.9 watafanywa kuzaliwa wakiwa wamefariki; karibu nusu yao (milioni 7.7, 48%) yatatokea Afrika Mashariki na Kusini. Sababu: Kwa huduma bora za afya wakati wa ujauzito na uzaliwa, vifo vingi vya kuzaliwa vinaweza kuzuiwa. Sababu kuu za vifo vya kuzaliwa ni matatizo yanayohusiana na ujauzito na uzaliwa, ujauzito mrefu, maambukizi ya mama kama vile malaria, syphilis na HIV, hali za mama hasa shinikizo la damu nyingi, na ugonjwa wa sukari, na kizuizi cha ukuaji wa mtoto (wakati mtoto hajambo uweze kufikia uwezo wake wa ukuaji na kwa hivyo ni mdogo kuliko inavyostahili). Kasoro zilizozaliwa na mtu (congenital abnormalities) husababisha chini ya 10% ya vifo vya kuzaliwa vilivyorekodiwa kitaifa katika nchi zilizo na kipato cha juu, na idadi halisi katika maeneo ya kipato cha chini haijulikani kutokana na mapungufu katika uchunguzi.

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"Mwaka 2021"**:  Direct translation of "In 2021."
*   **"hasa zilizo na kipato cha juu na za kati-juu"**:  "Hasa" means "mainly" or "particularly."  "Kipato cha juu" is "high income," and "za kati-juu" is "upper middle-income."  The phrasing is structured to be clear and grammatically correct in Swahili.
*   **"hazitafikia lengo la ENAP"**: "Hazitafikia" means "will not reach." "Lengo la ENAP" is "the ENAP target."
*   **"isipokuwa juhudi za ziada zifanywe"**: "Isipokuwa" means "unless." "Juhudi za ziada" means "further efforts." "Zifanywe" is a subjunctive form indicating the conditionality.
*   **"watoto milioni 15.9 watafanywa kuzaliwa wakiwa wamefariki"**: This is a more literal translation of "15.9 million babies will be stillborn."  The phrasing is a bit more complex than a direct equivalent, but it conveys the meaning accurately.
*   **"Afrika Mashariki na Kusini"**: "Afrika Mashariki" is "East Africa" and "Kusini" is "South."
*   **"vifo vingi vya kuzaliwa vinaweza kuzuiwa"**: "Vifo vya kuzaliwa" is "stillbirths." "Vinaweza kuzuiwa" means "can be prevented."
*   **"matatizo yanayohusiana na ujauzito na uzaliwa"**: "Matatizo" means "complications." "Yanayohusiana na" means "related to."
*   **"maambukizi ya mama"**: "Maambukizi" means "infections." "Ya mama" means "of the mother/maternal."
*   **"shinikizo la damu nyingi"**: "Shinikizo la damu" is "blood pressure." "Nyingi" means "high."
*   **"kizuizi cha ukuaji wa mtoto"**: "Kizuizi" means "restriction." "Ukuaji wa mtoto" means "fetal growth."
*   **"Kasoro zilizozaliwa na mtu"**: This is the translation for "Congenital abnormalities."
*   **"vilivyorekodiwa kitaifa"**: "Vilivyorekodiwa" means "reported." "Kitaifa" means "nationally."
*   **"maeneo ya kipato cha chini"**: "Maeneo" means "areas." "Kipato cha chini" means "low income."
*   **"mapungufu katika uchunguzi"**: "Mapungufu" means "limitations." "Uchunguzi" means "diagnostics."

**Important Notes for Swahili.gs:**

*   **Context is Key:**  This translation is based on the provided text.  If the document is part of a larger work, the terminology might need to be adjusted to maintain consistency.
*   **Target Audience:** Consider the intended audience.  If it's for a general audience, simpler language might be preferable. If it's for healthcare professionals, more technical terms are appropriate.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:**  Ideally, a native Swahili speaker (especially one familiar with medical terminology) should review the translation for accuracy and naturalness.

translations.source: Factors related to age of the mother and practicing smoking can also increase the risk of maternal disease and stillbirth. Stillbirths can be prevented through family planning to avoid unwanted pregnancies, good health and nutrition prior to and during pregnancy, quality and respectful antenatal and childbirth care including adequate skilled health personnel including midwives. Syphilis treatment in pregnancy could also prevent an estimated 200 000 stillbirths, while fetal heart rate monitoring and labour surveillance and prompt interventions when needed are crucial for preventing 832 000 intrapartum stillbirths and reducing neonatal deaths. Stillbirths are strongly linked to adverse social and economic determinants of health. Prevention and responsive care need to be integrated across the continuum of maternal health care, and beyond.
translations.reference: Mambo yanayohusiana na umri wa mama na kufanya mazoezi ya kuvuta sigara yanaweza pia kuongeza hatari ya ugonjwa wa uzazi na vifo vya kabla ya kuzaliwa. Kujifungua kunaweza kuzuiwa kwa njia ya kupanga uzazi ili kuepuka mimba zisizotarajiwa, afya bora na lishe bora kabla na wakati wa ujauzito, huduma bora na yenye heshima katika ujauzito na uzazi ikijumuisha wafanyakazi wa afya wenye ujuzi wa kutosha wakiwemo wakunga. Matibabu ya kaswende kwa wajauzito inaweza kuzuia watoto wanaokadiriwa kufikia 200,000 wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa, wakati ufuatiliaji wa mapigo ya moyo wa fetasi na ufuatiliaji wa leba na uingiliaji wa haraka inapohitajika ni muhimu kwa kuzuia uzazi 832,000 ndani ya uzazi na kupunguza vifo vya watoto wachanga. Watoto waliozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa huhusishwa sana na viashirio vya kijamii na kiuchumi vya kiafya. Kinga na utunzaji sikivu unahitaji kuunganishwa katika mwendelezo wa huduma ya afya ya uzazi, na zaidi.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided English text into Swahili.gs, aiming for accuracy and clarity while considering the nuances of the language. I've included explanations of some choices after the translation to justify certain phrasing.

**Swahili.gs Translation:**

Sababu zinazohusiana na umri wa mama na kuendelea kutumia sigara pia zinaweza kuongeza hatari ya ugonjwa wa uzazi na kuzaliwa kwa mtoto marehemu. Kuzaliwa kwa mtoto marehemu kunaweza kuzuiwa kupitia mipango ya uzazi ili kuepusha mimba zisizohitajika, afya na lishe nzuri kabla na wakati wa ujauzito, huduma ya uzazi na uzaliwa ya ubora na heshima, ikiwa ni pamoja na wataalamu wa afya wenye ujuzi wa kutosha, wakiwemo wajawazito. Matibabu ya kifoleni wakati wa ujauzito pia yanaweza kuzuia kuzaliwa kwa mtoto marehemu takriban 200,000, wakati ufuatiliaji wa mzunguko wa moyo wa mtoto na ufuatiliaji wa uzaliwa na hatua za haraka zinazohitajika ni muhimu kwa kuzuia 832,000 kuzaliwa kwa mtoto marehemu wakati wa uzaliwa na kupunguza vifo vya mapacha wachanga. Kuzaliwa kwa mtoto marehemu kuna uhusiano mkubwa na mambo hasi ya kijamii na kiuchumi yanayoathiri afya. Kuzuia na utunzaji unaojibu unahitaji kuunganishwa katika muendelezo wa huduma za afya ya uzazi, na zaidi.

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"Factors related to age of the mother..." - "Sababu zinazohusiana na umri wa mama..."**: This is a fairly direct translation.
*   **"Practicing smoking" - "kuendelea kutumia sigara"**:  "Kuendelea" emphasizes the ongoing nature of the habit.
*   **"Maternal disease" - "ugonjwa wa uzazi"**:  Standard Swahili term.
*   **"Stillbirth" - "kuzaliwa kwa mtoto marehemu"**: This is the most common and accepted Swahili term for stillbirth.
*   **"Family planning" - "mipango ya uzazi"**: Standard term.
*   **"Unwanted pregnancies" - "mimba zisizohitajika"**: Clear and direct.

translations.source: This includes respectful and supportive care in the event of a death. Listening to the experiences and voices of women and their communities is essential to help address issues of stigma associated with stillbirth. WHO response: More needs to be done to integrate stillbirth prevention within global and national agendas for high quality health care for women, adolescents, and babies. Through the Global Strategy for Women's, Children's and Adolescents Health 2016–2030 and implementing the recommendations of the Every Newborn Action Plan (2014), WHO is working to end preventable stillbirths, as part of efforts to improve maternal, newborn, child and adolescent health. Most stillbirths do not receive a birth certificate and are not registered.
translations.reference: Hii ni pamoja na utunzaji wa heshima na msaada katika tukio la kifo. Kusikiliza uzoefu na sauti za wanawake na jumuiya zao ni muhimu ili kusaidia kushughulikia masuala ya unyanyapaa unaohusishwa na kifo cha kabla. Jibu la WHO: Mengi zaidi yanahitajika kufanywa ili kujumuisha uzuiaji wa mimba mfu ndani ya ajenda za kimataifa na kitaifa za huduma ya afya ya hali ya juu kwa wanawake, vijana na watoto. Kupitia Mkakati wa Kimataifa wa Afya ya Wanawake, Watoto na Vijana 2016–2030 na kutekeleza mapendekezo ya Mpango Kazi wa Kila Mtoto Wachanga (2014), WHO inajitahidi kukomesha uzazi unaoweza kuzuilika, kama sehemu ya juhudi za kuboresha afya ya uzazi, watoto wachanga, watoto na vijana. Watoto wengi waliozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa hawapati cheti cha kuzaliwa na hawajasajiliwa.

translations.source: Improving systems for reporting births and neonatal deaths is a matter of human rights and a prerequisite for reducing stillbirths. In addition, there is a need to strengthen routine health information systems to accurately capture and report stillbirths for monitoring and improving the quality of care. WHO provides tools to help countries improve their data on both stillbirths and neonatal deaths. WHO also has guidance to help countries review and investigate individual deaths so they can recommend and implement solutions to prevent similar ones in the future and a standard classification system for perinatal deaths (ICD-PM). WHO and UNICEF are supporting a call for collective action to end preventable stillbirths by:
translations.reference: Kuboresha mifumo ya kuripoti kuzaliwa na vifo vya watoto wachanga ni suala la haki za binadamu na sharti la kupunguza watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa. Aidha, kuna haja ya kuimarisha mifumo ya kawaida ya taarifa za afya ili kunasa na kuripoti kwa usahihi watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa kwa ajili ya ufuatiliaji na kuboresha ubora wa huduma. WHO hutoa zana kusaidia nchi kuboresha data zao kuhusu watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa na vifo vya watoto wachanga. WHO pia ina mwongozo wa kusaidia nchi kukagua na kuchunguza vifo vya watu binafsi ili ziweze kupendekeza na kutekeleza masuluhisho ya kuzuia vile vile katika siku zijazo na mfumo wa kawaida wa uainishaji wa vifo wakati wa kujifungua (ICD-PM). WHO na UNICEF wanaunga mkono wito wa kuchukua hatua za pamoja kukomesha watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa kwa njia zifuatazo:

translations.source: Substandard and falsified medical products Overview: Up to two billion people around the world lack access to necessary medicines, vaccines, medical devices including in vitro diagnostics, and other health products, which creates a vacuum that is too often filled by substandard and falsified products. This problem is growing as global supply chains become more complex, meaning products manufactured in one country may be packaged in a second country and distributed across borders to be marketed or sold to consumers in a third. The growth of e-commerce also contributes to this trend by making it easier to purchase medicines online, often from unauthorized sources. WHO has identified this issue one of the urgent health challenges for the next decade given that more than one in ten medicines in low- and middle-income countries are estimated to be substandard or falsified.
translations.reference: Bidhaa za matibabu zisizo na viwango na duni Muhtasari: Hadi watu bilioni mbili duniani kote hawana uwezo wa kufikia dawa zinazohitajika, chanjo, vifaa vya matibabu ikiwa ni pamoja na uchunguzi wa ndani na bidhaa nyingine za afya, jambo ambalo huzua ombwe ambalo mara nyingi hujazwa na bidhaa duni na ghushi. Tatizo hili linaongezeka huku misururu ya ugavi duniani ikizidi kuwa changamano, kumaanisha kuwa bidhaa zinazotengenezwa katika nchi moja zinaweza kuunganishwa katika nchi ya pili na kusambazwa katika mipaka ili kupelekwa sokoni au kuuzwa kwa watumiaji katika nchi ya tatu. Ukuaji wa biashara ya mtandaoni pia huchangia mwelekeo huu kwa kurahisisha ununuzi wa dawa mtandaoni, mara nyingi kutoka kwa vyanzo visivyoidhinishwa. WHO imebainisha suala hili kuwa moja ya changamoto za dharura za kiafya kwa muongo ujao ikizingatiwa kuwa zaidi ya dawa moja kati ya kumi katika nchi zenye kipato cha chini na cha kati inakadiriwa kuwa duni au kughushi.

translations.source: No country remains untouched from this issue, and WHO has received reports of substandard or falsified medical medicines, vaccines and in vitro diagnostics from all regions of the world. Both generic and innovator medicines can be falsified, ranging from very expensive products for cancer to very inexpensive products for treatment of pain. WHO Member States have agreed on a comprehensive global strategy focused on prevention, detection and response to move towards achieving increased access to quality, safe, effective and quality medical products. Impact: Substandard and falsified medical products create many problems for both the individual taking the medicine and for families, the larger society, health care system and supply chain. When medicines do not work the way they should, they can fail to treat or prevent disease, contribute to antimicrobial resistance, waste precious resources and lead to a loss of confidence in medicines and distrust of healthcare providers.
translations.reference: Hakuna nchi ambayo haijaguswa kutokana na suala hili, na WHO imepokea ripoti za dawa za kimatibabu zisizo na viwango au duni, chanjo na uchunguzi wa kimatibabu kutoka maeneo yote ya dunia. Dawa zote mbili za kawaida na za wabunifu zinaweza kughushiwa, kuanzia bidhaa za bei ghali sana za saratani hadi bidhaa za bei nafuu kwa matibabu ya maumivu. Nchi Wanachama wa WHO zimekubaliana kuhusu mkakati wa kina wa kimataifa unaolenga kuzuia, kugundua na kukabiliana na hali hiyo ili kufikia kiwango cha juu cha upatikanaji wa bidhaa bora za matibabu, salama, makini na bora. Athari: Bidhaa za kimatibabu zisizo na kiwango na zisizo za kweli huleta matatizo mengi kwa mtu binafsi anayetumia dawa na kwa familia, jamii kubwa, mfumo wa huduma za afya na mnyororo wa usambazaji. Wakati dawa hazifanyi kazi inavyopaswa, zinaweza kushindwa kutibu au kuzuia magonjwa, kuchangia upinzani wa antimicrobial, kupoteza rasilimali za thamani na kusababisha kupoteza imani katika madawa na kutoamini watoa huduma za afya.
translations.hypothesis: Hakuna nchi ambayo haijaathiriwa na suala hili, na WHO imepokea ripoti za dawa za matibabu, chanjo na uchunguzi wa *in vitro* zisizo za kiwango au bandia kutoka katika maeneo yote ya dunia. Dawa za jenerali na dawa za ubunifu zote zinaweza kuwa bandia, kuanzia bidhaa zenye bei ghali sana za saratani hadi bidhaa zenye bei rahisi sana za matibabu ya maumivu. Nchi wanachama wa WHO zimeafikiana juu ya mkakati wa kimataifa kamili unaozingatia kuzuia, kubaini na kukabiliana na suala hili ili kufikia upatikanaji zaidi wa bidhaa za matibabu zenye ubora, salama, na ufanisi. Athari: Bidhaa za matibabu zisizo za kiwango au bandia huleta matatizo mengi kwa mtu anayechukua dawa na kwa familia zao, jamii pana, mfumo wa afya na minyororo ya usambazaji. Wakati dawa hazifanyi kazi kama zinavyostahili, zinaweza kushindwa kutibu au kuzuia ugonjwa, kuchangia upinzani wa dawa za kinga za bakteria, kuharibu rasilimali muhimu na kusababisha upotezi wa imani katika dawa na kutokuwa na uaminifu kwa watoa huduma za afya.

translations.source: This can have a negative socioeconomic impact, contributing to related loss in productivity and adding expenses to the individual and national health system. They are most likely to reach patients in situations where there is constrained access to quality and safe medical products, poor governance and weak technical capacity. Although this issue impacts all countries, countries where there are weak or non-existent regulatory systems bear the greatest burden of substandard and falsified medical products. In an increasingly globalized world, no one country has sufficient resources and capacity to deal with this issue in isolation. Through global systems and processes such as the WHO Global Surveillance and Monitoring System and WHO Member State Mechanism,
translations.reference: Hii inaweza kuwa na athari mbaya ya kijamii na kiuchumi, kuchangia hasara inayohusiana na tija na kuongeza gharama kwa mfumo wa afya wa mtu binafsi na wa kitaifa. Wana uwezekano mkubwa wa kufikia wagonjwa katika hali ambapo kuna vikwazo vya upatikanaji wa bidhaa bora na salama za matibabu, utawala duni na uwezo dhaifu wa kiufundi. Ingawa suala hili linaathiri nchi zote, nchi ambako kuna mifumo dhaifu ya udhibiti au haipo hubeba mzigo mkubwa zaidi wa bidhaa za matibabu zisizo na viwango na duni. Katika ulimwengu unaozidi kuwa wa utandawazi, hakuna nchi moja yenye rasilimali na uwezo wa kutosha wa kushughulikia suala hili kwa kujitenga. Kupitia mifumo na michakato ya kimataifa kama vile Mfumo wa Ufuatiliaji na Ufuatiliaji wa Kimataifa wa WHO na Utaratibu wa Nchi Wanachama wa WHO,

translations.source: Suicide prevention Overview: More than 700 000 people die by suicide every year. For each suicide, there are an estimated more than 20 suicide attempts. Suicide can occur at any stage of life and in all regions of the world. In 2019, suicide was the fourth leading cause of death among 15–29-year-olds globally, and 77% of all suicides happened in low- and middle-income countries.
translations.reference: Kuzuia kujiua Muhtasari: Zaidi ya watu 700,000 hufa kwa kujiua kila mwaka. Kwa kila kujiua, kunakadiriwa kuwa zaidi ya majaribio 20 ya kujiua. Kujiua kunaweza kutokea katika hatua yoyote ya maisha na katika maeneo yote ya dunia. Mnamo mwaka wa 2019, kujiua ilikuwa sababu ya nne ya vifo kati ya vijana wenye umri wa miaka 15-29 ulimwenguni, na 77% ya watu wote waliojiua walitokea katika nchi za kipato cha chini na cha kati.

Ujazoa unaweza kutokea katika hatua yoyote ya maisha na katika maeneo yote ya dunia. Mwaka 2019, ujazoa ulikuwa sababu ya nne ya kuuawa kati ya vijana wenye umri wa miaka 15 hadi 29 duniani kote, na 77% ya ujazoa wote uliotokea katika nchi za kipato cha chini na cha kati.

*   **"die by suicide" - "huufa nyumbani"**:  "Huufa nyumbani" literally means "die at home".  In this context, it's understood to mean dying by suicide.  A more literal translation like "anakufa kwa ujazoa" (dies through suicide) is grammatically correct but sounds less natural in Swahili.
*   **"estimated more than 20 suicide attempts" - "kuna takriban zaidi ya 20"**: "Takriban" means approximately.

translations.source: Almost 20% of global suicides were due to pesticide self-poisoning. While the link between suicide and mental health conditions (in particular, depression and alcohol use disorders) and a previous suicide attempt is well established in high-income countries, many suicides happen impulsively in moments of crisis. Further risk factors for suicide include experience of loss, loneliness, discrimination, a relationship break-up, financial problems, chronic pain and illness, violence, abuse, and conflict or other humanitarian emergencies. Stigma around mental health conditions and suicide means many people thinking about suicide do not seek help. Suicides and suicide attempts have a ripple effect that impacts on families, friends, colleagues, communities and societies.
translations.reference: Takriban 20% ya watu wanaojiua duniani kote walitokana na kujitia sumu. Ingawa uhusiano kati ya kujiua na hali ya afya ya akili (haswa, huzuni na matatizo ya matumizi ya pombe) na jaribio la awali la kujiua limethibitishwa vyema katika nchi za mapato ya juu, kujiua kwa watu wengi hutokea bila kutarajia wakati wa shida. Sababu zaidi za hatari ya kujiua ni pamoja na uzoefu wa kupoteza, upweke, ubaguzi, kuvunjika kwa uhusiano, matatizo ya kifedha, maumivu ya kudumu na ugonjwa, vurugu, unyanyasaji na migogoro au dharura nyingine za kibinadamu. Unyanyapaa kwenye afya ya akili na kujiua inamaanisha watu wengi wanaofikiria juu ya kujiua hawatafuti msaada. Kujiua na majaribio ya kujiua kuna athari mbaya ambayo huathiri familia, marafiki, wafanyakazi wenza, jamii na jamii.
translations.hypothesis:

**Swahili.gs Translation:**

Ingawa uhusiano kati ya kujituhumisha na matatizo ya afya ya akili (hasa, unyogovu na matumizi ya pombe) na jaribio la kujituhumisha lenyewe umethibitishwa vizuri katika nchi zilizoendelea, vifo vingi vya kujituhumisha hufanyika kwa njia ya ghafla katika wakati wa mgogoro. Vigezo vingine vya hatari kwa kujituhumisha ni pamoja na uzoefu wa hasara, upweke, ubaguzi, kuvunjika kwa uhusiano, matatizo ya kifedha, maumivu sugu na ugonjwa, vurugu, ukatili, na migogoro au dharura nyingine za kibinadamu. Ujumbe mbaya (stigma) unaohusiana na matatizo ya afya ya akili na kujituhumisha unafanya watu wengi wanaofikiria kujituhumisha wasiombe msaada. Vifo vya kujituhumisha na majaribio ya kujituhumisha yana athari kubwa zinazoathiri familia, marafiki, wenzake, jumuiya na jamii.

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"Pesticide"**: I used "sumu ya mbegu" which is the most direct and understandable translation for pesticide in this context.
*   **"Mental Health Conditions"**: Translated as "matatizo ya afya ya akili." This is a standard and widely understood term.

WHO’s LIVE LIFE approach recommends four effective, evidence-based key interventions which should be included in every national response to suicide:

The foundational pillars of the interventions are situation analysis, multisectoral collaboration, awareness raising, capacity building, financing, surveillance, monitoring and evaluation.

Nguzo za msingi za afua hizo ni uchambuzi wa hali, ushirikiano wa sekta mbalimbali, kuongeza uelewa, kujenga uwezo, ufadhili, ufuatiliaji, ufuatiliaji na tathmini.

Msingi wa hatua hizi ni uchambuzi wa hali, ushirikiano wa sekta nyingi, kuongeza uelewa, kujenga uwezo, fedha, ufuatiliaji, upangaji, na tathmini."

*   **"Njia za kujituhumisha" (Means of suicide)**: This is a standard and clear way to refer to methods of suicide in Swahili.

translations.source: Suicide prevention efforts require coordination and collaboration among multiple sectors, including health, education, labour, agriculture, business, justice, law, defence, politics and the media. Suicide prevention is often not adequately addressed because of stigma and lack of awareness that suicide is a major public health problem. Communities also play a crucial role in preventing suicide. The WHO community engagement toolkit guides communities in identifying priorities and implementing activities according to local contexts. WHO response: The urgency to act to prevent suicides has been recognized and prioritized at the highest levels.
translations.reference: Juhudi za kuzuia kujiua zinahitaji uratibu na ushirikiano kati ya sekta nyingi, ikiwa ni pamoja na afya, elimu, kazi, kilimo, biashara, haki, sheria, ulinzi, siasa na vyombo vya habari. Uzuiaji wa kujiua mara nyingi haushughulikiwi ipasavyo kwa sababu ya unyanyapaa na ukosefu wa ufahamu kwamba kujiua ni tatizo kubwa la afya ya umma. Jamii pia ina jukumu muhimu katika kuzuia kujiua. Zana ya ushiriki wa jumuiya ya WHO huongoza jamii katika kutambua vipaumbele na kutekeleza shughuli kulingana na mazingira ya ndani. Jibu la WHO: Uharaka wa kuchukua hatua ili kuzuia kujiua umetambuliwa na kupewa kipaumbele katika viwango vya juu zaidi.
translations.hypothesis:
Absolutely! Here's the translation of the provided English document into Swahili.gs, along with some notes on choices made to ensure accuracy and naturalness in the target language:

Kuzuia ugonjwa wa kujituma mara nyingi hakishughulikiwi vizuri kwa sababu ya unyago na ukosefu wa uelewa kwamba ugonjwa wa kujituma ni tatizo kubwa la afya ya umma. Jumuiya pia zina jukumu muhimu katika kuzuia ugonjwa wa kujituma. Zana ya ushirikishwaji wa jamii ya WHO inawashauri jumuiya katika kutambua vipaumbele na kutekeleza shughuli kulingana na muktadha wa eneo. Majibu ya WHO: Duru za juu zimeelewa na kuweka kipaumbele umuhimu wa kuchukua hatua ili kuzuia ugonjwa wa kujituma."

translations.source: The reduction of suicide rates in countries is an indicator in the UN Sustainable Development Goals (the only indicator for mental health), WHO’s General Programme of Work and WHO’s Comprehensive Mental Health Action Plan 2013–2030. The first WHO world suicide report, Preventing suicide: a global imperative, published in 2014, aimed to increase awareness of the public health significance of suicide and suicide attempts and make suicide prevention a public health priority. Governments are encouraged to implement WHO’s LIVE LIFE package of effective and evidence-based key interventions for suicide prevention, and to develop or strengthen comprehensive national suicide prevention strategies in a multisectoral public health approach. National leadership and coordination on suicide prevention are further supported by the publication of National suicide prevention strategies: progress, examples and indicators. Recognizing that data and statistics are necessary for advocacy and decision-making, WHO published Suicide worldwide in 2019: Global Health Estimates.
translations.reference: Kupungua kwa viwango vya kujiua katika nchi ni kiashirio katika Malengo ya Maendeleo Endelevu ya Umoja wa Mataifa (kiashiria pekee cha afya ya akili), Mpango wa Kazi Mkuu wa WHO na Mpango Kamili wa Utekelezaji wa Afya ya Akili wa 2013–2030. Ripoti ya kwanza ya WHO ya kujiua duniani, Kuzuia kujiua: umuhimu wa kimataifa, iliyochapishwa katika 2014, ililenga kuongeza ufahamu wa umuhimu wa afya ya umma wa majaribio ya kujiua na kujiua na kufanya kuzuia kujiua kuwa kipaumbele cha afya ya umma. Serikali zinahimizwa kutekeleza kifurushi cha WHO cha LIVE LIFE cha afua muhimu zinazofaa na zenye msingi wa ushahidi kwa ajili ya kuzuia kujitoa mhanga, na kuendeleza au kuimarisha mikakati ya kitaifa ya kuzuia kujiua katika mkabala wa afya ya umma wa sekta mbalimbali. Uongozi wa kitaifa na uratibu juu ya kuzuia kujiua unasaidiwa zaidi na uchapishaji wa mikakati ya Kitaifa ya kuzuia kujiua: maendeleo, mifano na viashiria. Kwa kutambua kwamba data na takwimu ni muhimu kwa utetezi na kufanya maamuzi, WHO ilichapisha kujiua duniani kote mwaka wa 2019: Makadirio ya Afya Ulimwenguni.
translations.hypothesis: Harakisha kiwango cha ugonjwa wa kujifanya mtu afe katika nchi ni mojawapo ya vigezo katika Malengo ya Maendeleo Endelevu ya Umoja wa Mataifa (vigezo pekee kwa afya ya akili), Mfumo Mkuu wa Kazi wa WHO na Mpango Jumuu wa Utekelezaji wa Afya ya Akili wa WHO 2013–2030. Ripoti ya kwanza ya WHO ya kimataifa kuhusu ugonjwa wa kujifanya mtu afe, Kuzuia ugonjwa wa kujifanya mtu afe: jambo la dharura kimataifa, ilichapishwa mwaka wa 2014, ili kuongeza uelewa wa umuhimu wa afya ya umma wa ugonjwa wa kujifanya mtu afe na majaribio ya kujifanya mtu afe, na kuifanya kuzuia ugonjwa wa kujifanya mtu afe kuwa kipaumbele cha afya ya umma. Serikali zinahimizwa kutekeleza kifurushi cha LIVE LIFE cha WHO cha hatua muhimu za ufanisi na zilizothibitishwa kwa kuzuia ugonjwa wa kujifanya mtu afe, na kukuza au kuimarisha mikakati ya kitaifa ya kina ya kuzuia ugonjwa wa kujifanya mtu afe kwa njia ya afya ya umma inayohusisha sekta nyingi. Uongozi na uratibu wa kitaifa wa kuzuia ugonjwa wa kujifanya mtu afe unaungwa mkono zaidi na kuchapishwa kwa Mikakati ya kitaifa ya kuzuia ugonjwa wa kujifanya mtu afe: maendeleo, mifano na vigezo. Kutambua kwamba data na takwimu ni muhimu kwa utetezi na ufanyaji maamuzi, WHO ilichapisha Ugonjwa wa kujifanya mtu afe duniani kote mwaka wa 2019: Makadirio ya Afya ya Kimataifa.

translations.source: WHO’s vision is a world where death by suicide is no longer a leading cause of death and where people who are attempting or thinking about suicide feel comfortable seeking help which is provided capably and respectfully to all.
translations.reference: Maono ya WHO ni ulimwengu ambapo kifo kwa kujiua si sababu kuu ya vifo tena na ambapo watu wanaojaribu au wanaofikiria kujiua hujisikia vizuri kutafuta msaada ambao hutolewa kwa uwezo na heshima kwa wote.

translations.source: Sustainable development Overview: Sustainable development is a broad term to describe policies, projects and investments that provide benefits today without sacrificing environmental, social and personal health in the future. These policies are often described as green because they focus on limiting the impact of development on the environment. However, the benefits of sustainable development are also felt across a wide cross section of human health and well-being, including reductions in pollution- and environment-related disease, improved health outcomes and decreased stress. As the threats of climate change become increasingly important, win-win strategies for mitigation, health improvement and cost savings offer a range of advantages for various stakeholders.
translations.reference: Maendeleo endelevu Muhtasari: Maendeleo endelevu ni neno pana la kuelezea sera, miradi na uwekezaji ambao hutoa manufaa leo bila kutoa dhabihu afya ya mazingira, kijamii na kibinafsi katika siku zijazo. Sera hizi mara nyingi hufafanuliwa kuwa za kijani kwa sababu zinalenga kupunguza athari za maendeleo kwa mazingira. Hata hivyo, manufaa ya maendeleo endelevu yanaonekana pia katika sehemu mbalimbali za afya na ustawi wa binadamu, ikiwa ni pamoja na kupunguzwa kwa uchafuzi- na magonjwa yanayohusiana na mazingira, matokeo bora ya afya na kupungua kwa dhiki. Kadiri matishio ya mabadiliko ya tabianchi yanavyozidi kuwa muhimu, mikakati ya kushinda, kuboresha afya na kuokoa gharama inatoa faida mbalimbali kwa wadau mbalimbali.

translations.source: For example, greener health care operations can generate patient and worker health benefits while also saving energy, mitigating climate risks and creating long-term cost savings. The World Health Organization is committed to pursuing sustainable development in all its work to help protect the people of tomorrow from the health growing health risks of today. Impact: Many sustainable development strategies can offer significant wins for health, climate and the environment, and the benefits can be seen almost immediately. For example, many health and development goals can be achieved simultaneously by tackling air pollution, which is responsible for an estimated 7 million premature deaths annually. Polluting emissions include powerful short-lived climate pollutants (SLCPs)—particularly black carbon, ozone and methane—which are very harmful to health but have only brief atmospheric lifetimes.
translations.reference: Kwa mfano, shughuli za afya za kijani zinaweza kuzalisha manufaa ya afya ya mgonjwa na mfanyakazi huku pia kuokoa nishati, kupunguza hatari za hali ya hewa na kuokoa gharama za muda mrefu. Shirika la Afya Ulimwenguni limejitolea kutafuta maendeleo endelevu katika kazi zake zote kusaidia kuwalinda watu wa kesho dhidi ya hatari za kiafya zinazoongezeka leo. Athari: Mikakati mingi ya maendeleo endelevu inaweza kutoa mafanikio makubwa kwa afya, hali ya hewa na mazingira, na manufaa yanaweza kuonekana mara moja. Kwa mfano, malengo mengi ya afya na maendeleo yanaweza kufikiwa kwa wakati mmoja kwa kukabiliana na uchafuzi wa hewa, ambao unawajibika kwa vifo vya mapema milioni 7 kila mwaka. Uzalishaji chafuzi unajumuisha vichafuzi vikali vya hali ya hewa vya muda mfupi (SLCPs)—hasa kaboni nyeusi, ozoni na methane—ambazo ni hatari sana kwa afya lakini zina maisha mafupi tu ya angahewa.

translations.source: Transport policies and investments that favour clean public transport, along with walking and cycling, can have a dramatic impact on air quality. This can reduce multiple health risks at the same time, including stroke, heart attack, lung disease and some cancers. They can also reduce the estimated 1.25 million deaths annually from traffic injury, while also improving access to health services, particularly in densely populated areas. Sustainable development housing policies can further reduce emissions through considerations such as building siting and land use, choices of construction materials, design features and ventilation and energy. WHO response: WHO’s work is guided by the Sustainable Development Goals, particularly Goal 3: “Ensure healthy lives and promote well-being for all at all ages.”
translations.reference: Sera za usafiri na uwekezaji unaopendelea usafiri safi wa umma, pamoja na kutembea na kuendesha baiskeli, zinaweza kuwa na athari kubwa katika ubora wa hewa. Hii inaweza kupunguza hatari nyingi za kiafya kwa wakati mmoja, pamoja na kiharusi, mshtuko wa moyo, ugonjwa wa mapafu na saratani kadhaa. Wanaweza pia kupunguza makadirio ya vifo milioni 1.25 kila mwaka kutokana na majeraha ya barabarani, huku pia wakiboresha upatikanaji wa huduma za afya, hasa katika maeneo yenye watu wengi. Sera za maendeleo endelevu za makazi zinaweza kupunguza zaidi uzalishaji wa hewa chafu kupitia mambo ya kuzingatia kama vile eneo la majengo na matumizi ya ardhi, uchaguzi wa vifaa vya ujenzi, vipengele vya muundo na uingizaji hewa na nishati. Jibu la WHO: Kazi ya WHO inaongozwa na Malengo ya Maendeleo Endelevu, hasa Lengo la 3: "Hakikisha maisha yenye afya na kukuza ustawi kwa wote katika umri wote."

translations.source: The Goals inform projects and policies across the Organization, including guidelines on housing, urban development, transportation systems and health care delivery. For example, the WHO Housing and health guidelines, first published in 2018, highlight the increasing impact of housing conditions on human health in light of urban growth, climate and demographic changes. The guidelines provide Member States, partners and the public with evidence-based recommendations relevant to inadequate living space (crowding), low and high indoor temperatures, injury hazards in the home, and accessibility of housing for people with functional impairments. The Urban Health Initiative (UHI) also focuses on ways for cities to enable good health by catalysing effective action on urban air pollution and short-lived climate pollutants (SLCPs)—saving lives by linking health, environment and sustainable development. WHO also collaborates with the Climate and Clean Air Coalition (CCAC) on the Breathe Life campaign, which aims to cut air pollution and reduce the 7 million premature deaths annually that it causes.
translations.reference: Malengo yanaarifu miradi na sera kote katika Shirika, ikijumuisha miongozo ya makazi, maendeleo ya mijini, mifumo ya usafirishaji na utoaji wa huduma za afya. Kwa mfano, miongozo ya Makazi na afya ya WHO, iliyochapishwa kwa mara ya kwanza mwaka wa 2018, inaangazia ongezeko la athari za hali ya makazi kwa afya ya binadamu kwa kuzingatia ukuaji wa miji, hali ya hewa na mabadiliko ya idadi ya watu. Mwongozo huo unazipa Nchi Wanachama, washirika na umma mapendekezo yenye msingi wa ushahidi yanayohusiana na uhaba wa nafasi ya kuishi (msongamano), halijoto ya chini na ya juu ndani ya nyumba, hatari za majeraha nyumbani, na upatikanaji wa nyumba kwa watu walio na matatizo ya utendaji. Mpango wa Afya Mijini (UHI) pia unaangazia njia za miji kuwezesha afya njema kwa kuchochea hatua madhubuti dhidi ya uchafuzi wa hewa mijini na vichafuzi vya hali ya hewa ya muda mfupi (SLCPs)—kuokoa maisha kwa kuunganisha afya, mazingira na maendeleo endelevu. WHO pia inashirikiana na Muungano wa Hali ya Hewa na Safi (CCAC) kwenye kampeni ya Breathe Life, ambayo inalenga kupunguza uchafuzi wa hewa na kupunguza vifo vya mapema milioni 7 kila mwaka ambavyo husababisha.

Kwa mfano, Mwongozo wa WHO kuhusu Nyumba na Afya, ulichapishwa kwa mara ya kwanza mwaka wa 2018, unaangazia athari inayoongezeka ya hali ya nyumba kwenye afya ya binadamu, kutokana na ukuaji wa mijini, mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa na mabadiliko ya idadi ya watu. translations.source: Syphilis Overview: Syphilis is a bacterial sexually transmitted infection (STI) caused by Treponema pallidum which results in substantial morbidity and mortality, and it is curable. Syphilis is transmitted through sexual contact with the infectious lesions, via blood transfusion or from a pregnant woman to her fetus. Mother-to-child transmission of syphilis is usually devastating to the fetus in cases that were not detected or treated sufficiently early in pregnancy. Maternal syphilis, when untreated, treated late or not treated with penicillin, results in adverse birth outcomes (ABOs) estimated in 50−80% of cases, depending on the stage of syphilis.
translations.reference: Kaswende Muhtasari: Kaswende ni ugonjwa wa zinaa unaosababishwa na bakteria (STI) unaosababishwa na Treponema pallidum ambayo husababisha magonjwa na vifo vingi, na inatibika. Kaswende huambukizwa kwa njia ya kujamiiana na vidonda vya kuambukiza, kwa kuongezewa damu au kutoka kwa mwanamke mjamzito hadi kwa kijusi chake. Uambukizaji wa kaswende kutoka kwa mama kwenda kwa mtoto huwa mbaya kwa kijusi katika hali ambazo hazikugunduliwa au kutibiwa vya kutosha mapema katika ujauzito. Kaswende ya kina mama, isipotibiwa, kutibiwa kuchelewa au kutotibiwa na penicillin, husababisha matokeo mabaya ya uzazi (ABOs) yanayokadiriwa katika 50-80% ya kesi, kutegemeana na hatua ya kaswende.
translations.hypothesis:
