translations.source: “To stay on track towards the 2030 SDG agenda, we must act decisively and collectively to deliver a measurable impact in all countries.” This year’s report includes for the first time a dedicated section on climate change and health, and we anticipate that this will be of more relevance in the report going forward. For this issue and all other areas timely, reliable and disaggregated data are critical to track progress and improve national and global health policies. Editor’s note: The World Health Statistics report is the World Health Organization’s (WHO) annual compilation of the most recent available data on health and health-related indicators. For inquiries, contact [email protected]
translations.reference: "Ili kukaa kwenye mstari kuelekea ajenda ya 2030 ya SDG, lazima tuchukue hatua madhubuti na kwa pamoja ili kuleta athari inayoweza kupimika kwa nchi zote" Ripoti ya mwaka huu inajumuisha kwa mara ya kwanza sehemu maalumu kuhusu mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa na afya, na tunatarajia kwamba hii itakuwa na umuhimu zaidi katika ripoti inayoendelea. Kwa suala hili na maeneo mengine yote kwa wakati, Data ya kuaminika na iliyogawanywa ni muhimu kufuatilia maendeleo na kuboresha sera za afya za kitaifa na kimataifa. Dokezo la mhariri: Ripoti ya takwimu za afya Duniani ni mkusanyo wa kila mwaka wa shirika la afya Duniani (WHO) wa Data zinazopatikana hivi karibuni kuhusu afya na viashirio vinavyohusiana na afya. Kwa maswali, wasiliana na [barua pepe imehifadhiwa]

translations.source: WHO Director-General announces Global Health Leaders Awards Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum and Peter Piot awarded for lifelong commitment to health WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus will present two lifetime achievement awards to recognize outstanding contributions to advancing global health during the high-level opening session of the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly. The Director-General’s Global Leaders Awards, which were established in 2019, will go to Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum and Peter Piot. Both recipients are distinguished scientists and public health leaders who were closely involved in the discovery of Ebola virus disease before advancing to leadership positions in global health.
translations.reference: Mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO ataoangoza tuzo za viongozi wa afya Duniani Jean-Jacques-Muyembe-Tamfum na Peter Piot walitunukiwa tuzo ya kujitolea kwa maisha yote kwa afya Mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus atatoa tuzo mbili za mafanikio ya maisha ili kutambua michango bora katika kuendeleza afya Duniani wakati wa ufunguzi wa ngazi ya juu wa mkutano wa Sabini na sita wa afya Duniani. Tuzo za mkurugenzimkuu kwa tuzo za viongozi Duniani, ambazo zilianzishwa mwaka 2019, zitakwenda kwa Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum na Peter Piot. Wapokeaji wote ni wanasayansi mashuhuri na viongozi wa afya ya umma ambao walihusika kwa karibu katika ugunduzi wa virusi vya Ebola kabla ya kusonga mbele hadi nyadhifa za uongozi katika afya ya kimataifa.

translations.source: The award honours their lifetime achievements in public health. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is being recognized for his innovation, leadership and ground-breaking contributions to the management and treatment of diseases such as Ebola virus disease. He has played a critical role in improving health outcomes in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, the African region, and the global community. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum is the Director General of the DRC’s National Institute of Biomedical Research in Kinshasa, full Professor of Microbiology at the University of Kinshasa Medical School and the inaugural president of the Congolese Academy of Science. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum was part of the team that discovered the Zaïre ebolavirus during the first recorded outbreak in 1976, in Equateur Province, Central DRC.
translations.reference: Tuzo hiyo inaheshimu mafanikio yao ya maisha katika afya ya umma. Profesa Muyembe-Tamfum kutoka Jamhuri ya kidemokrasia ya Kongo (DRC) anatambulika kwa uvumbuzi, uongozi na michango yake ya msingi katika usimamizi na matibabu ya magonjwa kma vile ugonjwa wa Ebola. Amekuwa na jukumu muhimu katika kuboresha matokeo ya afya katika Jamhuri ya kidemokrasia ya kongo, kanda ya Afrika, na jumuia ya kimataifa. Profesa Muyembe-Tamfum ni mkurugenzi mkuu wa taasisi ya kitaifa ya utafiti wa Tiba kibiolojia ya DRC huko Kinshasa, profesa kamili wa Biolojia katika chuo kikuu cha Kinshasa shule ya afya na Rais wa kuanzishwa kwa chuo kikuu cha Sayansi cha Kongo. Profesa Muyembe-Tamfum alikuwa sehemu ya timu iliyogundua virusi vya Ebola vya Zaire wakati wa mlipuko wa kwanza uliorekodiwa mnmo 1976, katika jimbo la Ikweta, katikati mwa DRC.
translations.hypothesis: Hata hivyo, tuzo hii inathibitisha mafanikio yao ya maisha yote katika afya ya umma. Profesa Muyembe-Tamfum kutoka Jamhuri ya Kidemokrasia ya Kongo (DRC) ananongeshwa kwa ubunifu wake, uongozi na michango yake ya msingi katika usimamizi na matibabu ya magonjwa kama vile ugonjwa wa virusi vya Ebola. Amecheza jukumu muhimu katika kuboresha matokeo ya afya nchini Jamhuri ya Kidemokrasia ya Kongo, eneo la Afrika, na jumuiya ya kimataifa. Profesa Muyembe-Tamfum ni Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa Taasisi ya Kitaifa ya Utafiti wa Kibayolojia ya DRC mjini Kinshasa, Profesa Kamili wa Microbiology katika Shule ya Sayansi ya Matibabu ya Chuo Kikuu cha Kinshasa na Rais wa kwanza wa Chuo Kisaikolojia cha Kongo. Profesa Muyembe-Tamfum alikuwa miongoni mwa timu iliyoendelea kugundua virusi vya Zaïre ebolavirus wakati wa mlipuko wa kwanza uliorekodiwa mwaka wa 1976, katika Mkoa wa Equateur, DRC ya Kati.

translations.source: He later helped design one of the first effective treatments against the virus and the deployment of experimental Ebola virus disease vaccines. He has worked tirelessly to build the Democratic Republic of the Congo’s scientific capacity, establishing multiple research facilities in the country, and has played key roles with WHO in fighting infectious diseases. Professor Piot from Belgium is being recognized for his extraordinary leadership in the response to the HIV/AIDS epidemic and other significant global health challenges. He has played a vital role in launching major global health initiatives such as UNAIDS, the Global Fund, and others that have improved health outcomes around the world. Under his leadership, UNAIDS became the chief advocate for worldwide action against AIDS, also spearheading UN reform by bringing together 10 different UN organizations.
translations.reference: Baadae alisaidia kubuni moja wapo ya matibabu ya kwanza ya ufanisi dhidi ya virusi na kupelekwa kwa chanjo ya majaribio ya ugonjwa wa Ebola. Amefanya kazi bila kuchoka kujenga uwezo wa kisayansi wa Jamhuri ya kidemokraia ya Kongo, kuanzisha vituo vingi vya utafiti nchini humo, na amefanya majukumu muhimu na WHO katika kupambana na magonjwa ya kuambukiza. Profesa Piot kutoka Ubelgiji anatambuliwa kwa uongozi wa ajabu katika kukabiliana na janga la VVU/ UKIMWI na changamoto nyingine muhimu za afya Duniani. Amekuwa na jukumu muhimu katika kuzindua mipango mikuu ya afya ya kimataifa kama vile UNAIDS, mfuko wa Dunia, na mengineyo ambayo imeboresha matokeo ya afya Duniani kote. Chini ya uongozi wake, UNAIDS imekuwa mtetezi mkuu wa hatua za kimataifa dhidi ya UKIMWI, pia akiongoza mageuzi kwa kuyaleta pamoja mashirika 10 tofauti ya umoja wa mataifa.

translations.source: Professor Piot also played a part in the earlier discovery of the Ebola virus, and the containment of the first known outbreak, participating in field epidemiological investigations. He has chaired a number of high-level initiatives, served in numerous high-level advisory roles, including on COVID-19, and has received multiple awards. Professor Piot is the Special Advisor to the EC President, a Professor of Global Health and former Director of the London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine. “These outstanding individuals embody lifelong dedication, relentless advocacy, a commitment to equity, and selfless service of humanity and people’s health,” said WHO Director-General, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “In a time of multiple emergencies, health crises, and conflict, we recognize that leadership in public health and science are more important than ever to advance global health.
translations.reference: Pia profesa Piot alishiriki katika ugunduzi wa awali wa virusi vya Ebola, na kuzuia mlipuko wa kwanza ulipojulikana, alishiriki katika uga wa uchunguzi wa magonjwa. Ameongoza idadi ya mipango ya ngazi ya juu, alihudumu katika majukumu mengi ya ngazi ya juu ya ushauri, ikiwa ni pamoja na UVIKO-19 na amepokea tuzo nyingi. Profesa Piot ni mshauri muhimu wa Rais wa EC, profesa wa afya Ulimwenguni na mkurugenzi wa zamani wa shule ya London ya uafi na tiba ya kitropiki. "Watu hawa mashuhuri wanajumuisha kujitolea kwa maisha yote, utetezi usio na kuchoka, kujitolea kwa usawa, na huduma ya kujitolea kwa Ubinadamu na afya ya watu", alisema Mkurugunzi mkuu wa WHO, Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. "Katika wakati wa dharura nyingi, migogoro ya afya, na migogoro tunatambua kwamba uongozi katika afya ya umma na sayansi ni muhimu zaidi kuliko hapo awali ili kuendeleza afya Duniani.
translations.hypothesis:

Profesa Piot pia alihusika katika ugunduzi wa awali wa virusi vya Ebola, na udhibiti wa mlipuko wa kwanza ulioripotiwa, kwa kushiriki katika tafiti za epidemiolojia za uwanja. Ameongoza mipango mbalimbali ya ngazi ya juu, amekuwa mshauri katika majukumu mengi ya ngazi ya juu, ikiwa ni pamoja na kuhusu COVID-19, na amepokea tuzo nyingi. Profesa Piot ni Mshauri Maalum kwa Rais wa EC, Profesa wa Afya ya Kimataifa, na Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa zamani wa Shule ya Afya ya Joto na Tropiki ya London. “Watu hawa wa kipekee wanaakisi kujitolea maisha yote, ulinzi usiozimia, kujitolea kwa usawa, na huduma ya kibinaadamu na afya ya watu bila kujali,” alisema Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa WHO, Dkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “Katika zama za dharura nyingi, viko vyeupe vya afya, na migogoro, tunatambua kwamba uongozi katika afya ya umma na sayansi ni muhimu zaidi kuliko hapo kabla ili kukuza afya ya kimataifa.”

translations.source: Our awardees today personify that passion.” “Their combined stories and successes show how much the world has progressed in terms of collaboration at the global, regional and country levels to strengthen health emergency preparedness, response and resilience. These outstanding scientists will continue to inspire us every day,” he added The ceremony for the awards form part of the high-level opening session of the 76th World Health Assembly being held on Sunday 21 May 2023 in Geneva.
translations.reference: Washindi wetu leo wanawakilisha shauku hiyo" "Simulizi na mafanikio yao ya pamoja yanaonyesha jinsi ulimwengu umepiga hatua katika suala la ushirikiano katika ngazi ya kimataifa, kikanda na nchi ili kuimarisha maandalizi ya dharura ya afya, majibu na ustahimilivu. Wanasayansi hawa mashuhuri wataendelea kukutia moyo", aliongezea sherehe ya tuzo hizo ni sehemu ya kikao cha ngazi ya juu cha ufunguzi wa mkutano wa 76 wa afya Duniani unaofanyika Jumapili tarehe 21 Mei 2023 huko Jeniva.
translations.hypothesis: Hata washindi wetu wa leo huwakilisha shauku hiyo.” "Hadithi na mafanikio yao yaliyochanganywa yanaonyesha jinsi dunia ilivyofanya maendeleo katika suala la ushirikiano katika ngazi za kimataifa, kikanda na kitaifa ili kuimarisha utayari, majibu na ulinzi dhidi ya dharura za afya. Wanasayansi hawa wa kipekee watatuendelea kutupaongoza kila siku," aliongeza. Sherehe ya tuzo hizo ni sehemu ya kikao cha ufunguzi wa ngazi za juu wa Mkutano wa 76 wa Afya Duniani unaofanyika siku ya Jumapili, Mei 21, 2023, jiji Geneva.

translations.source: WHO congratulates Benin and Mali for eliminating trachoma as a public health problem WHO has validated Benin and Mali as having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem, making them the fifth and sixth countries in WHO’s African Region to achieve this significant milestone. Countries that previously received WHO validation for trachoma elimination are Ghana (June 2018), Gambia (April 2021), Togo (May 2022) and Malawi (September 2022). “WHO congratulates the health authorities of Benin and Mali and their network of global and local partners for these milestones”, said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “Following Benin’s and Mali’s success, trachoma remains endemic in 23 countries in WHO’s African Region, bringing us a step closer towards the elimination target for trachoma set in the road map for neglected tropical diseases 2021–2030.”
translations.reference: WHO inazipongeza Benini na Mali kwa kutokomeza Trakoma kama tatizo la afya ya umma. WHO imeziidhinisha Benini na Mali kama zimetokomeza Trakoma kama tatizo la afya ya umma, na kuzifanya kuwa nchi za tano na sita katika kanda ya Afrika ya WHO kufikia hatua hii muhimu. Nchi ambazo hapo awali zilipokea uthibitisho wa WHO wa kutokomeza Trakoma ni Ghana (Juni 2018), Gambia (Aprili 2021), Togo(Mei 2022) na Malawi (Septemba 2022). "WHO inazipongeza mamlaka za afya za Benini na Mali na mtandao wao wa washirika wa kimataifana wa ndani kwa hatua hizi muhimu", alisema Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO. "Kufuatia mafanikio ya Benini na Mali, Trakoma imesalia kuwa ugonjwa katika nchi 23 katika kanda ya Afrika ya WHO, na kutuletea hatua karibu na lengo la kutokomeza Trakoma lililowekwa katika muongozo wa magonjwa ya kitropiki yaliyosahaulika 2021-2022".
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Swahili.gs translation of the provided document:

WHO inaishukuru Benin na Mali kwa kuondoa trachoma kama tatizo la afya ya umma. WHO imethibitisha kuwa Benin na Mali zimeondoa trachoma kama tatizo la afya ya umma, na hivyo kuwa nchi ya tano na sita katika Mkoa wa WHO wa Afrika kufikia hatua muhimu hii. Nchi zilizothibitishwa hapo awali na WHO kwa kuondoa trachoma ni Ghana (Juni 2018), Gambia (Aprili 2021), Togo (Mei 2022) na Malawi (Septemba 2022). “WHO inaishukuru viongozi wa afya wa Benin na Mali na mtandao wao wa washirika wa kimataifa na wa ndani kwa hatua hizi,” alisema Dkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa WHO. “Kufuatia mafanikio ya Benin na Mali, trachoma bado ni endelevu katika nchi 23 katika Mkoa wa WHO wa Afrika, na hivyo kutuleta karibu na lengo la kuondoa trachoma lililowekwa katika ramani ya barabara ya magonjwa ya kipinga ya trobati 2021–2030.”

translations.source: Globally, Benin and Mali join 15 other countries that have been validated by WHO for having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem. These are Cambodia, China, the Gambia, Ghana, Islamic Republic of Iran, Lao People’s Democratic Republic, Malawi, Mexico, Morocco, Myanmar, Nepal, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Togo and Vanuatu. Trachoma elimination using WHO’s SAFE strategy Both Benin and Mali implemented the WHO-recommended SAFE strategy to eliminate trachoma with the support of WHO and partners. The SAFE strategy consists of surgery to treat late trachoma complications; antibiotics to clear infection; facial cleanliness; and environmental improvement, particularly improving access to water and sanitation, to reduce transmission.
translations.reference: Duniani kote, Benini na Mali zinaungana na nchi nyingine 15 ambazo zimethibitishwa na WHO kwa kutokomeza Trakoma kama tatizo la afya ya umma. Nchi hizo ni, Kambodia, China, Gambia, Ghana, Jamhuri ya Kiislamu ya Irani, Jamhuri ya kidemokrasia ya watu wa Lao,Malawi, Meksiko, Moroko, Myanmar, Nepali, Omani, Saudi Arabia, Togo na Vanuatu. Utokomezaji wa Trakoma kwa kutumia mkakati SALAMA wa WHO Benini na Mali zote zilitekeleza makakti SAFE uliopendekezwa na WHO ili kuondoa Trakoma kwa msaada wa WHO na washirika. Mkakati SAFE unajumuisha upasuaji wa kutibu matatizo ya Trakoma ya muda mrefu; viuavidudu ili kuondoa maambukizi; usafi wa uso; na uboreshaji wa usafi wa mazingira, hasa kuboresha upatikanaji wa maji na usafi wa mazingira, ili kupunguza maambukizi.
translations.hypothesis:
Kimataifa, Benin na Mali zinaungana na nchi 15 nyingine zilizothibitishwa na WHO kwamba zimeondoa trachoma kama tatizo la afya ya umma. Nchi hizi ni Kamboja, China, Gambia, Ghana, Jamhuri ya Kiislamu ya Iran, Lao People’s Democratic Republic, Malawi, Mexico, Morocco, Myanmar, Nepal, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Togo na Vanuatu.

Kuondoa trachoma kwa kutumia mkakati wa WHO’s SAFE. Benin na Mali zote zilitumia mkakati wa SAFE uliopendekezwa na WHO ili kuondoa trachoma kwa msaada wa WHO na washirika. Mkakati wa SAFE unajumuisha upasuaji kutibu matatizo ya trachoma ya baadaye; dawa za kuua vijasumu kuondoa maambukizi; usafi wa uso; na uboreshaji wa mazingira, hasa kuongeza upataji wa maji na usafi, ili kupunguza usambazaji.

translations.source: Through the International Trachoma Initiative, the antibiotic azithromycin is donated by Pfizer to elimination programmes implementing the SAFE strategy. Benin has integrated trachoma elimination interventions with those implemented against other neglected tropical diseases (NTDs), under the umbrella of the National Programme for Communicable Diseases. Trachoma is the third NTD to be eliminated in Benin, after dracunculiasis (in 2009) and gambiense human African trypanosomiasis (in 2021). Mali has conducted trachoma impact and surveillance surveys and rolled out interventions to achieve elimination targets, despite security challenges in the northern regions of the country and sociopolitical upheavals in recent years. Trachoma is the first NTD to be eliminated in Mali, which therefore now joins a global group of 47 countries that have eliminated at least one NTD.
translations.reference: Kupitia mpango wa kimataifa wa Trakoma, dawa ya Azithromycin inatolewa na Pfizer kwa programu za kutokomeza zinazotekeleza mkakati SALAMA. Benini imeunganisha afua za kutokomeza Trakoma na zile zinazotekelezwa dhidi ya magonjwa mengine ya kitropiki yaliyosahaulika (NTDs), chini ya mwamvuli wa mpango wa kitaifa wa magonjwa ya kuambukiza. Trakoma ni NTD ya tatu kuondolewa nchini Benini, baada ya Dracunculiasis (mwak 2009) na Trypanosomiasisi ya Binadamu ya Afrika ya Gambia (mwaka 2021). Mali imefanya tafiti za athari za Trakoma na ufuatiliaji na kuzindua afua ili kufikia malengo ya utomezaji, licha ya changamoto za kiusalama katika mikoa ya kaskazini mwa nchi na misukumo ya kijamii na kisiasa katika miaka ya hivi karibuni. Trakoma ni NTD ya kwanza kutokomezwa nchini Mali, ambayo pia sasa inaungana na kundi la kimataifa la nchi 47 ambazo zimetokomeza angalau NTD moja.

Benin imechanganya hatua za kuondoa trachoma na hatua zilizotekelezwa dhidi ya magonjwa mengine ya kigeni yasiyoyashughulikiwa (NTDs), chini ya programu ya kitaifa ya magonjwa yanayoenezwa. Trachoma ni ugonjwa wa NTD wa tatu kuondolewa nchini Benin, baada ya dracunculiasis (mwezi wa 2009) na gambiense binadamu African trypanosomiasis (mwezi wa 2021). Mali imefanya tafiti za athari na ufuatiliaji wa trachoma na imeanza hatua za kufikia malengo ya kuondoa ugonjwa huu, licha ya changamoto za usalama katika maeneo ya kaskazini ya nchi na matatizo ya kijamii na siasa katika miaka ya hivi karibuni. Trachoma ni ugonjwa wa NTD wa kwanza kuondolewa nchini Mali, na hivyo sasa inajiunga na kundi la nchi 47 duniani zilizondoa ugonjwa wa NTD angalau mmoja.**

translations.source: “These are impressive public health achievements,” said Dr Ibrahima Socé Fall, Director of the WHO Global NTD Programme. “Benin and Mali demonstrate how strong political will, cross-sector integration, surveillance and community engagement can work in concert to achieve disease elimination.” Significant progress has been made in the fight against trachoma over the past few years. The number of people requiring antibiotic treatment for trachoma in the WHO African Region fell by 84 million, from 189 million in 2014 to 105 million as of June 2022. Note to the editor: Trachoma remains a public health problem in 41 countries (as of June 2022) with an estimated 125 million people living in areas requiring interventions against the disease.
translations.reference: "Haya ni mafanikio ya afya ya umma", alisema Dk Ibrahima Soce Fall, mkurugenzi wa mpango wa kimataifa wa NTD wa WHO. "Benini na Mali zinaonyesha jinsi dhamira thabiti ya kisiasa, ushirikiano wa sekta mbalimbali, ufuatiliaji na ushirikishaji wa jamii inayoweza kufanya kazi kwa pamoja ili kufikia utokomezaji wa magonjwa". Mafanikio makubwa yamepatikana katika vita dhidi ya Trakoma katika miaka michache iliyopita. Idadi ya watu wanaohitaji matibabu ya viuavijasumu kwa Trakoma katika kanda ya Afrika ya WHO ilipungua kwa milioni 84, kutoka milioni 189 mwaka 2014 hadi milioni 105 kufikia Juni 2022. Maelezo kwa mhariri: Trakoma bado ni tatizo la afya ya umma katika nchi 41 (hadi Juni 2022) huku takribani watu milioni 125 wakiishi katika maeneo yanayohitaji afua dhidi ya ugonjwa huo.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Swahili.gs translation of the provided English document, aiming for accuracy and natural flow:

“Haya ni mafanikio makubwa ya afya ya umma,” alisema Dkt Ibrahima Socé Fall, Mkurugenzi wa Programu ya Kimataifa ya WHO ya Magonjwa Yasiyoambukiza (NTD). “Benini na Mali zinaonyesha jinsi nia thabiti ya kisiasa, ujumuishaji wa sekta mbalimbali, ufuatiliaji na ushirikishwaji wa jamii vinaweza kufanya kazi pamoja ili kufikia uondoaji wa ugonjwa.” Maendeleo makubwa yamefanyika katika mapambano dhidi ya trachoma katika miaka ya hivi karibuni. Idadi ya watu wanao hitaji wa matibabu ya kinga ya bakteria kwa trachoma katika Kanda ya Afrika ya WHO ilipungua milioni 84, kutoka milioni 189 mnamo mwaka wa 2014 hadi milioni 105 ifikapo Juni 2022. Kumbuka kwa mhariri: Trachoma bado ni tatizo la afya ya umma katika nchi 41 (ifikapo Juni 2022) na watu milioni 125 wanaoishi katika maeneo yanayohitaji hatua za kuzuia ugonjwa huu.”

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **Formal Language:** I've used a formal register appropriate for a public health announcement.
*   **WHO:**  I kept "WHO" as is, as it's a widely recognized acronym.
*   **NTD:** Similarly, "NTD" (Neglected Tropical Diseases) is kept as is.  A full translation would be cumbersome and less common in this context.
*   **"Inaonyesha" vs. "Huonyesha":** I used "inaonyesha" (demonstrates) which is more appropriate for a specific instance.
*   **"Kufikia" vs. "mpaka":** "Kufikia" (reaching/as of) is more precise than "mpaka" (until) in this context.
*   **Numbers:** Numbers are translated into Swahili format.
*   **Clarity:** I aimed for clarity and readability, ensuring the meaning is conveyed accurately.

translations.source: Trachoma is found mainly in the poorest and most rural areas of Africa, Central and South America, Asia, the Western Pacific and the Middle East. The WHO African Region is disproportionately affected by trachoma with 105 million people living in at-risk areas, which represents 84% of the global trachoma burden. Trachoma is the leading infectious cause of blindness worldwide. The disease is caused by infection with the bacterium Chlamydia trachomatis. Infection is transmitted from person to person through contaminated fingers, fomites and flies that have come into contact with discharge from the eyes or nose of an infected person.
translations.reference: Trakoma hupatikana hasa katika maeneo ya maskini zaidi na ya vijijini zaidi ya Afrika, Amerika ya kati na Kusini, Asia, Pasifiki ya Magharibi na Mashariki ya kati. WHO kanda ya Afrika imeathiriwa kwa kiasi kikubwa na Trakoma huku watu milioni 105 wakiishi katika maeneo hatarishi, ambayo inawakilisha 84% ya wingi wa Trakoma Duniani. Trakoma ni chanzo kikuu cha maradhi ya upofu Duniani kote. Ugonjwa husababishwa kwa kuambukizwa na bakteria Clamydia Trachomatis. Maambukizi huama kutoka kwa mtu hadi kwa mtu kupitia vidole vilivyochafuliwa, Fomites na nzi ambao wamegusana na uchafu kutoka katika macho au pua ya mtu aliyeambukizwa.

Trachoma huonekana hasa katika maeneo maskini zaidi na ya vijijini barani Afrika, Amerika ya Kati na Kusini, Asia, Bahari ya Magharibi na Mashariki, na Mashariki ya Kati. Eneo la WHO la Afrika linaathiriwa kwa kiasi kikubwa na trachoma, na watu milioni 105 wanaishi katika maeneo yenye hatari, ambayo yanaashiria 84% ya mzigo wa trachoma duniani. Trachoma ndiyo sababu kuu ya ulemavu wa macho unaosababishwa naambukizo ulimwenguni. Ugonjwa huu unasababishwa naambukizo na bakteria Chlamydia trachomatis.ambukizo huenezwa kutoka mtu hadi mtu kupitia vidole vichafu, vitu vinavyotumika kwa pamoja, na pepo ambazo zimekuja kugusa mzio kutoka macho au pua ya mtu aliyeambukizwa.

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

translations.source: Environmental risk factors for trachoma transmission include poor hygiene, overcrowded households, inadequate access to water or use of proper sanitation facilities. In 1996, WHO launched the WHO Alliance for the Global Elimination of Trachoma by the year 2020 (GET2020). With other partners in the Alliance, WHO supports country implementation of the SAFE strategy and strengthening of national capacity through epidemiological assessment, monitoring, surveillance, project evaluation and resource mobilization contributing towards elimination of trachoma as a public health problem. The road map, endorsed by the World Health Assembly in 2020 through its decision 73(33), sets 2030 as the new target date for global elimination.
translations.reference: Mambo hatarishi ya kimazingira kwa maambukizi ya Trakoma ni pamoja na hali duni ya usafi, kaya zenye msongamano mkubwa wa watu, kutopata maji ya kutosha au matumizi ya vifaa vya usafi wa mazingira. Mnamo 1996, WHO ilizindua muungano wa WHO wa kutokomeza Trakoma Duniani kufikia mwaka 2020 (GET 2020). Pamoja na washirika wengine katika muungano, WHO inaunga mkono utekelezaji wa mkakati SAFE nchini na uimarishaji wa uwezo wa kitaifa kupitia tathmini ya magonjwa, usimamiaji, ufuatiliaji, tathmini ya mradi na uhamasishaji wa rasilimali unaochangia kutokomeza Trakoma kama tatizo la afya ya umma. Muongozo, ulioidhinishwa na baraza la afya Duniani mwaka 2020 kupitia uamuzi wake wa 73(33), inaweka 2030 kama tarehe mpya inayolengwa ya kutokomezwa Duniani.
translations.hypothesis:

Mwaka 1996, WHO ilianzisha Muungano wa WHO kwa Kuondoa kabisa Trachoma duniani ifikapo mwaka 2020 (GET2020). Pamoja na washirika wengine wa Muungano, WHO inasaidia nchi katika kutekeleza mkakati wa SAFE na kuimarisha uwezo wa kitaifa kupitia tathmini ya epidemiolojia, ufuatiliaji, uelekeaji, tathmini ya mradi na uhamasishaji wa rasilimali, kuchangia katika kuondoa trachoma kama tatizo la afya ya umma. Ramani ya mwelekeo, iliyothibitishwa na Baraza la Afya la Dunia mwaka 2020 kupitia uamuzi wake wa 73(33), imewekwa tarehe 2030 kama lengo jipya la kuondoa kabisa trachoma duniani.

translations.source: 152 million babies born preterm in the last decade Of every 10 babies born, 1 is preterm – and every 40 seconds, 1 of those babies dies Preterm birth rates have not changed in the past decade in any region of the world The impacts of conflict, climate change, COVID-19 are increasing risks for women and babies everywhere Action is needed urgently to improve the prevention of preterm birth, alongside better care for affected babies and their families
translations.reference: Watoto milioni 152 walizaliwa njiti katika muongo uliopita Kati ya watoto 10 wanaozaliwa, 1 ni njiti - na kila sekunde 40, mtoto 1 kati ya hao hufariki Viwango vya kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati havijabadilika katika muongo mmoja uliopita katika eneo lolote la Dunia. Athari za migogoro, mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa, UVIKO-19 zinaongeza hatari kwa wanawake na watoto kila mahali. Hatua zinahitajika haraka ili kuboresha uzuiaji wa kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati, pamoja na utunzaji bora kwa watoto walioathiriwa na familia zao.

translations.source: An estimated 13.4 million babies were born pre-term in 2020, with nearly 1 million dying from preterm complications, according to a new report released by United Nations agencies and partners today. This is equivalent to around 1 in 10 babies born early (before 37 weeks of pregnancy) worldwide. Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth, produced by the World Health Organization (WHO) and the United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF) together with PMNCH -- the world’s largest alliance for women, children, and adolescents, sounds the alarm on a “silent emergency” of preterm birth, long under-recognized in its scale and severity, which is impeding progress in improving children’s health and survival. The report includes updated estimates from WHO and UNICEF, prepared with the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine, on the prevalence of preterm births. Overall, it finds that preterm birth rates have not changed in any region in the world in the past decade, with 152 million vulnerable babies born too soon from 2010 to 2020.
translations.reference: Inakadiriwa watoto milioni 13.4 walizaliwa Njiti mwaka 2020, na karibu milioni 1 wakifa kutokana na matatizo ya unjiti, kulingana na ripoti mpya iliyotolewa leo na mashirika ya umoja wa mataifa. Hii ni sawa na mtoto 1 kati ya 10 aliyezaliwa mapema (kabla ya wiki 37 za ujauzito) Duniani kote. Alizaliwa hivi karibuni: Muongo wa hatua juu ya kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati, iliyotolewa na shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) na shirika la Umoja wa mataifa la kuhudumia watoto (UNICEF) pamoja na PMNCH - muungano mkubwa zaidi Duniani wa wanawake, watoto, na vijana, inapiga kengele kwenye "Dharura ya kimya kimya" ya kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati, ambayo haijatambuliwa kwa muda mrefu katika kiwango na ukali wake, ambayo inazuia maendeleo katika kuboresha afya na maisha ya watoto. Ripoti hiyo inajumuisha makadirio yaliyosasishwa kutoka WHO na UNICEF, iliyotayarishwa na shule ya London ya usafi na madawa ya kitropiki, kuhusu kuenea kwa watoto wanaozaliwa kabla ya wakati. Kwa ujumla, ripoti inagundua kuwa viwango vya kuzaliwa Njiti havijabadilika katika eneo lolote Duniani katika muongo mmoja uliopita, na watoto milioni 152 walio katika mazingira magumu walizaliwa hivi karibuni kutoka 2010 hadi 2020.
translations.hypothesis: Hata takriban milioni 13.4 ya watoto walizaliwa mapema mwaka 2020, na karibu milioni 1 wakufa kutokana na matatizo ya kuzaliwa mapema, kulingana na ripoti mpya iliyotolewa na mashirika ya Umoja wa Mataifa na washirika wao leo. Hii ni sawa na takriban 1 kati ya watoto 10 waliozaliwa mapema (kabla ya wiki 37 za mimba) duniani kote. "Kuzaliwa mapema: muongo wa hatua juu ya kuzaliwa mapema," iliyoandaliwa na Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) na Shirika la Watoto la Umoja wa Mataifa (UNICEF) pamoja na PMNCH - muungano mkubwa zaidi duniani kwa wanawake, watoto, na vijana, inazua tahadhari kuhusu "dharura ya kimya" ya kuzaliwa mapema, ambayo kwa muda mrefu haijatambikizwa kwa ukubwa wake na ukali wake, na inazuia maendeleo katika kuboresha afya na ulinzi wa watoto. Ripoti inajumuisha makadirio yaliyosasishwa kutoka WHO na UNICEF, yaliotayarishwa na Shule ya Sayansi ya Afya ya London na Dawa, kuhusu ukubwa wa kuzaliwa mapema. Kwa ujumla, inakuta kuwa viwango vya kuzaliwa mapema havijabadilika katika eneo lolote la ulimwengu katika muongo uliopita, na milioni 152 ya watoto wanao hatari walizaliwa mapema kuanzia 2010 hadi 2020.

translations.source: Preterm birth is now the leading cause of child deaths, accounting for more than 1 in 5 of all deaths of children occurring before their 5th birthday. Preterm survivors can face lifelong health consequences, with an increased likelihood of disability and developmental delays. Survival gaps by region, income, race Building from a landmark report on the topic in 2012, this new Born too soon “decade” report provides a comprehensive overview of the prevalence of preterm birth and its profound impact on women, families, societies and economies. Too often, where babies are born determines if they survive.
translations.reference: Kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati sasa ndio sababu kuu ya vifo vya watoto, ikichukua zaidi ya 1 kati ya vifo 5 vya watoto vinavyotekea kabla ya miaka 5 ya kuzaliwa. Waathirika wa kuzaliwa Njiti hukabiliwa na matokeo ya afya ya maisha yote, na kuongezeka uwezekano wa ulemavu na uchelewaji wa maendeleo. Mapengo ya kuishi kwa kanda, kipato, rangi Kutokana na ripoti ya kihistoria kuhusu mada mnamo 2012, ripoti hii mpya ya "Muongozo" wa kuzaliwa hivi karibuni inatoa muhtasari wa kina wa kuenea kwa kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati na athari zake kwa wanawake, familia, jamii na uchumi. Mara nyingi mno, mahali ambapo watoto huzaliwa huamua kama wataishi.

**Uzalendo mapema sasa ni sababu inayoongoza ya vifo vya watoto, ikichukua zaidi ya moja kati ya watoto wanne wa vifo vyote vya watoto vinavyotokea kabla ya umri wa miaka mitano. Watoto walioishiwa uzalendo mapema wanaweza kukabiliana na matokeo ya kiafya ya kudumu, na uwezekano wa ziada wa ulemavu na kuchelewesha maendeleo. Tofauti za ulinzi kulingana na eneo, kipato, na kabila Kuanzia ripoti muhimu kuhusu mada hiyo mnamo 2012, ripoti hii mpya ya "miaka kumi" ya "Born too soon" hutoa muhtasari kamili wa kuenea kwa uzalendo mapema na athari yake kubwa kwa wanawake, familia, jamii, na uchumi. Mara nyingi, wapi mtoto anazaliwa huamua kama atabaki hai.**

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **Uzalendo mapema:** This is the standard Swahili term for "preterm birth."
*   **Sababu inayoongoza:** "Leading cause" is translated as "sababu inayoongoza" (leading cause).

translations.source: The report notes that only 1 in 10 extremely preterm babies (<28 weeks) survive in low-income countries, compared to more than 9 in 10 in high-income countries. Gaping inequalities related to race, ethnicity, income, and access to quality care determine the likelihood of preterm birth, death, and disability, even in high-income countries. Southern Asia and sub-Saharan Africa have the highest rates of preterm birth, and preterm babies in these regions face the highest mortality risk. Together, these two regions account for more than 65% of preterm births globally. The report also highlights that the impacts of conflict, climate change and environmental damage, COVID-19, and rising living costs are increasing risks for women and babies everywhere.
translations.reference: Ripoti hiyo inabainisha kuwa ni mtoto 1 kati ya 10 wanaozaliwa mapema kabla ya wakati (chini ya wiki 28) wanaishi katika nchi zenye kipato cha chini, ikilinganishwa na zaidi ya 9 kati ya 10 katika nchi zenye kipato cha juu. Pengo la ukosefu wa usawa kuhusiana na rangi, kabila, mapato, ufikiaji wa huduma bora huamua uwezekano wa kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati, kifo, na ulemavu, hata katika nchi zenye kipato cha juu. Kusini mwa Asia na Afrika kusini mwa jangwa la Sahara ndizo zinazo viwango vya juu zaidi vya kuzaliwa Njiti, na watoto wachanga katika maeneo haya wanakabiliwa na hatari kubwa zaidi ya vifo. Kwa pamoja, maeneo haya mawili yanachaangia zaidi ya 65% ya watoto wanaozaliwa kabla ya wakati wao kukamilika Duniani. Pia ripoti hiyo inaangazia kwamba athari za migogoro, mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa, na uharibifu wa mazingira, UVIKO-19, na kupanda kwa gharama za maisha kunaongeza hatari kwa wanawake na watoto kila mahali.

Asia Kusini na Afrika ya Mashariki yana viwango vya juu zaidi vya kuzaliwa mapema, na watoto waliozaliwa mapema katika maeneo haya wanakabiliwa na hatari kubwa zaidi ya kufa. Ripoti pia inaangazia kuwa athari za migogoro, mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa na uharibifu wa mazingira, COVID-19, na kuongezeka kwa gharama za maisha zinaongeza hatari kwa wanawake na watoto kila mahali.

translations.source: For example, air pollution is estimated to contribute to 6 million preterm births each year. Nearly 1 in 10 preterm babies are born in the 10 most fragile countries affected by humanitarian crises, according to a new analysis in the report. Maternal health risks, such as adolescent pregnancy and pre-eclampsia, are closely linked to preterm births. This underlines the need to ensure access to sexual and reproductive health services, including effective family planning, with high-quality care in pregnancy and around the time of birth. Agenda for action: more country investment and parent-led activism

Karibu mtoto 1 kati ya 10 anayezaliwa kabla ya wakati analiwa katika nchi 10 dhaifu zaidi zilizoathiriwa na majanga ya kibinadamu, kulingana na uchambuzi mpya katika ripoti hiyo. Hatari za afya ya Uzazi, kama vile mimba za utotoni na pre-eclampsia, zinahusishwa kwa karibu na kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati. Hii inasisitiza haya ya kuhakikisha upatikanaji wa huduma za afya ya ngono na uzazi, ikiwa ni pamoja na upangaji uzazi bora, na utunzaji wa hali ya juu wakati wa ujauzito na wakati wa kujifungua. Ajenda ya utekelezaji: uwekezaji zaidi wa nchi na harakati zinazoongozwa na wazazi

translations.source: The past decade has also seen a growth of community activism on preterm birth and stillbirth prevention, driven by networks of parents, health professionals, academia, civil society, and others. Throughout the world, groups for affected families of preterm birth have been at the forefront of advocating for access to better care and policy change and supporting other families. Leading up to the International Maternal Newborn Health Conference, in Cape Town, South Africa from 8-11 May, WHO, UNICEF, UNFPA and PMNCH are calling for the following actions to improve care for women and newborns and mitigate risks from preterm births: Increased investments: Mobilizing international and domestic resources to optimize maternal and newborn health, ensuring high quality care is provided when and where it is needed. Accelerated implementation: Meeting country targets for progress through implementing established national policies for maternal and newborn care.
translations.reference: Pia muuongo uliopita umeshuhudia ukuaji wa harakati wa jamii juu ya kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati na kuzuia uzazi, unaoendeshwa na mitandao ya wazazi, wataalamu wa afya, wasomi, Asasi za kiraia, na wengine. Kote ulimwenguni, Makundi ya familia zilizoathiriwa za kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati wao wamekuwa mstari wa mbele kutetea upatikanaji wa matunzo bora na mabadiliko ya sera na kusaidia familia nyingine. Kuelekea kongamano la kimataifa la afya ya mama wajawazito, huko Cape Town, Afrika Kusini, kuanzia tarehe 8-11 Mei, WHO, UNICEF, UNFPA na PMMNCH wanatoa wito wa kuchukua hatua zifuatazo ili kuboresha huduma kwa wanawake na watoto wachanga na kupunguza hatari zinazotokana na kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati: Ongezeko la uwekezaji: Kuhamasisha rasilimali za kimataifa na za ndani ili kuboresha afya ya uzazi na watoto wachanga, kuhakikisha utunzaji wa hali ya juu unatolewa wakati na mahali unapohitajika. Kuharakisha utekelezaji: Kufikia malengo ya nchi kwa ajili ya maendeleo kupitia sera zilizowekwa za kitaifa za utunzaji wa uzazi na watoto wachanga.

translations.source: Integration across sectors: Promoting education through the life cycle; supporting smarter economic investments, with co-financing across sectors; strengthening climate adaptation responses across the life-course; and advancing the coordination and resilience of emergency systems. Locally driven innovation: Investing in locally led innovation and research to support improvements in quality of care and equity in access. Quotes: Prof. Joy Lawn, London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine Co-lead, Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth (2023) and Born too soon (2012) "This new report shows that the cost of inaction over the last decade was 152 million babies born too soon.
translations.reference: Ushirikiano katika sekta zote: Kukuza elimu kupitia mzunguko wa maisha; kusaidia uwekezaji mzuri wa kiuchumi; ufadhili wa pamoja katika sekta zote; kuimarisha majibu ya kukabiliana na hali ya hewa katika kipindi chote cha maisha; na kuendeleza uratibu na uthabiti wa mifumo ya dharura. Uvumbuzi unaoendeshwa ndani ya nchi: Kuwekeza katika uvumbuzi na utafiti unaoongozwa na ndani ili kusaidia uboreshaji wa ubora wa utunzaji na usawa katika ufikiaji. Nukuu: Profesa Joy Lawn, Shule ya London ya usafi na Tiba ya kitropiki Kiongozi mwenza, Alizaliwa hivi karibuni: Muongo wa hatua juu ya kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati (2023) na kuzaliwa hivi karibuni (2012) "Ripoti hii mpya inaonyesha kuwa gharama ya kutochukua hatua katika muongo uliopita ilikuwa watoto milioni 152 walizaliwa mapema sana.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Swahili.gs translation of the provided English document, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the nuances of the language. I've included explanations after the translation to justify some choices.

**Swahili.gs Translation:**

Ushirikiano baina ya sekta mbalimbali: Kukuza elimu kupitia mzunguko wa maisha; kusaidia uwekezaji wa kiuchumi wenye akili, na ushirikiano wa fedha baina ya sekta; kuimarisha majibu ya kukabiliana na mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa kupitia mzunguko wa maisha; na kuendeleza uratibu na uimara wa mifumo ya dharura.  Uboreshaji unaotokana na jamii: Kuwekeza katika uboreshaji na utafiti unaoongozwa na jamii ili kusaidia uboreshaji wa ubora wa huduma na usawa katika upataji. Nukuu: Prof. Joy Lawn, Shule ya Afya ya Umma na Starehe ya London, Mwenyekiti Mshiriki, Alizaliwa mapema: muongo wa hatua juu ya kuzaliwa mapema (2023) na Alizaliwa mapema (2012) "Ripoti hii mpya inaonyesha kuwa gharama ya kutofanya choo katika muongo uliopita ilikuwa watoto milioni 152 waliozaliwa mapema."

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"Integration across sectors" - "Ushirikiano baina ya sekta mbalimbali":**  "Ushirikiano" is a good general term for integration, emphasizing collaboration. "Baina ya" means "between." "Sekta mbalimbali" means "various sectors."
*   **"Promoting education through the life cycle" - "Kukuza elimu kupitia mzunguko wa maisha":** "Kukuza" means "to promote." "Mzunguko wa maisha" is the standard Swahili translation for "life cycle."
*   **"Supporting smarter economic investments, with co-financing across sectors" - "Kusaidia uwekezaji wa kiuchumi wenye akili, na ushirikiano wa fedha baina ya sekta":** "Uwekezaji wa kiuchumi" is "economic investments." "Wenye akili" translates to "smart" or "intelligent." "Ushirikiano wa fedha" means "financial co-financing."
*   **"Strengthening climate adaptation responses across the life-course" - "Kuimarisha majibu ya kukabiliana na mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa kupitia mzunguko wa maisha":** "Kuimarisha" means "to strengthen." "Majibu ya kukabiliana" means "adaptation responses." "Mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa" is "climate change."
*   **"Advancing the coordination and resilience of emergency systems" - "Kuendeleza uratibu na uimara wa mifumo ya dharura":** "Kuendeleza" means "to advance." "Uratibu" means "coordination." "Uimara" means "resilience." "Mifumo ya dharura" means "emergency systems."
*   **"Locally driven innovation" - "Uboreshaji unaotokana na jamii":** This was a tricky one.  "Uboreshaji" means "improvement" or "innovation." "Unaotokana na jamii" means "originating from the community" or "community-driven."  It captures the essence of "locally driven."
*   **"Investing in locally led innovation and research" - "Kuwekeza katika uboreshaji na utafiti unaoongozwa na jamii":** "Kuwekeza" means "to invest." "Utafiti" means "research." "Unaoongozwa na jamii" means "led by the community."
*   **"Quotes: Prof. Joy Lawn..." - "Nukuu: Prof. Joy Lawn...":** "Nukuu" is the standard word for "quote."
*   **"Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth" - "Alizaliwa mapema: muongo wa hatua juu ya kuzaliwa mapema":** "Alizaliwa mapema" means "born too soon." "Muongo wa hatua" means "decade of action." "Kuzaliwa mapema" means "preterm birth."
*   **"This new report shows that the cost of inaction over the last decade was 152 million babies born too soon." - "Ripoti hii mpya inaonyesha kuwa gharama ya kutofanya choo katika muongo uliopita ilikuwa watoto milioni 152 waliozaliwa mapema."** "Ripoti hii mpya" means "this new report." "Inaonyesha" means "shows." "Gharama ya kutofanya choo" means "the cost of inaction." "Uliopita" means "last."

**Important Notes for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  Swahili.gs is a developing language, and the best translation can depend heavily on the specific context.
*   **Community Review:** Ideally, this translation should be reviewed by native Swahili speakers, especially those familiar with the subject matter (health, economics, etc.).

translations.source: While some regions are more affected, preterm birth threatens health progress in every country. Greater investment in the care of vulnerable newborns can save millions of families from heartbreak. More work is also needed to prevent preterm birth, which will also improve progress in reducing stillbirths and maternal deaths. Together these twin tracks of preterm prevention and care will produce healthier individuals and societies to deliver on economic and social development. Our next generation depends on us all acting now -- the investment may not be small but the returns on this investment will be major for every country"
translations.reference: Wakati baadhi ya maeneo yameathiriwa zaidi, uzazi wa mapema unatishia maendeleo ya kiafya katika kila nchi. Uwekezaji mkubwa katika utunzaji wa watoto wachanga walio katika mazingira magumu unaweza kuokoa mamilioni ya familia kutokana na huzuni. Pia kazi zaidi inahitajika ili kuzuia kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati, ambayo pia itaboresha maendeleo katika kupunguza vifo vya uzazi na vifo vya watoto wachanga. Kwa pamoja kauli mbizi hizi za kuzuia na kutunzaa kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati zitazalisha watu binafsi na jamii zenye afya zaidi ili kuleta maendeleo ya kiuchumi na kijamii. Kizazi chetu kijacho kinatutegemea sisi sote kuchukua hatua sasa -- uwekezaji unaweza usiwe mdogo lakini faida ya uwekezaji huu itakuwa kubwa kwa nchi"

**Swahili.gs Translation:**

Ingawa baadhi ya maeneo yanaugua zaidi, kuzaliwa mapema kinatishia maendeleo ya afya katika kila nchi. Uwekezaji mkubwa katika huduma za watoto wachanga wenye hatari unaweza kuokoa familia milioni kadhaa kutokana na huzuni. Kijana wetu wa kizazi kijacho kinategemea sisi sote kuchukua hatua sasa – uwekezaji huu hauwezi kuwa mdogo, lakini faida za uwekezaji huu zitakuwa kubwa kwa kila nchi.

*   **"Preterm birth"**: I translated this as "kuzaliwa mapema" which literally means "early birth." This is the standard and most readily understood term in Swahili for preterm birth.

translations.source: Dr Nahya Salim, Tanzania: Born Too Soon co-author and national lead of Newborn Essential Solutions and Technologies 360 (NEST360) “There is now no excuse for silence. We now have in our hands the devices and the knowledge to change outcomes for our most vulnerable newborns. I am proud to see my government now investing and committed to implementation across even rural areas. The next decade can and must be different for those facing preterm birth here and everywhere.”

translations.source: Dr Anshu Banerjee, Director for Maternal, Newborn, Child and Adolescent Health and Ageing at WHO “Ensuring quality care for these tiniest, most vulnerable babies and their families is absolutely imperative for improving child health and survival. Progress is also needed to help prevent preterm births -- this means every woman must be able to access quality health services before and during pregnancy to identify and manage risks.” Steven Lauwerier, Director of Health (a.i.), UNICEF “After every preterm death is a trail of loss and heartbreak.

Anshu Banerjee, Mkurugenzi wa Afya ya Mama, Mtoto, Vijana na Wanaume Wazima katika WHO, amesema, “Kuhakikisha huduma bora kwa watoto hawa wadogo sana na wanyonge na familia zao ni jambo la muhimu sana kwa kuboresha afya na ulinzi wa watoto. Pia, maendeleo yanahitajika ili kusaidia kuzuia kuzaliwa mapema – hili maana yake ni kwamba kila mwanamke lazima aweze kupata huduma bora za afya kabla na wakati wa ujauzito ili kubaini na kudhibiti hatari.” Steven Lauwerier, Mkurugenzi wa Afya (m.k.), UNICEF, amesema, “Baada ya kifo cha kila mtoto aliyezaliwa mapema, kuna mnyororo wa hasara na moyo uliovunjika.”

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **"Dr."**: Translated as "Dkt." - standard Swahili abbreviation.

*   **"Maternal, Newborn, Child and Adolescent Health and Ageing"**: This was a complex phrase. I've translated it as "Afya ya Mama, Mtoto, Vijana na Wanaume Wazima" which is a common and understandable way to express this in Swahili.
*   **"WHO"**: Left as "WHO" as it's a widely recognized international abbreviation.

*   **"(a.i.)"**: Translated as "(m.k.)" which stands for "wakala mkuu" - meaning "acting"
*   **"Trail of loss and heartbreak"**: Translated as "mnyororo wa hasara na moyo uliovunjika" - meaning "a chain of loss and a broken heart." This aims to capture the emotional weight of the original.
*   **"UNICEF"**: Left as "UNICEF" as it's a widely recognized international abbreviation.

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

translations.source: Despite the many advances the world has made in the past decade, we have made no progress in reducing the number of small babies born too soon or averting the risk of their death. The toll is devasting. It’s time we improve access to care for pregnant mothers and preterm infants and ensure every child gets a healthy start and thrives in life.” Helga Fogstad, Executive Director, PMNCH “Born too soon underlines why we must increase investment and accountability for preterm birth – the world’s largest cause of death of children under the age of five.
translations.reference: Licha ya maendele mengi ambayo ulimwengu umefanya katika muongo mmoja uliopita, hataujafanya maendeleo yoyote katika kupunguza idadi ya watoto wadogo wanaozaliwa mapema au kuepusha hatari ya kifo chao. Uhuru ni mbaya. Ni wakati wa kuboresha upatikanaji wa huduma kwa mama wajawazito na watoto wachanga wanaozaliwa kabla ya wakati na kuhakikisha kila mtoto anapata mwanzo mzuri na kustawi maishani" Helga Fogstad, mkurugenzi mtendaji, PMNCH "Kuzaliwa mapema kunasisitiza kwa nini ni lazima kuongeza uwekezaji na uwajibikaji kwa kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati, sababu kubwa zaidi ya vifo vya watoto chini ya umri wa miaka mitano, Duniani.
translations.hypothesis:

Athari yake ni ya majeraha makubwa. Ni wakati wa kuboresha upataji wa huduma kwa wajawazito na watoto wachanga waliozaliwa mapema, na kuhakikisha kila mtoto anaanza maisha kwa afya na kustawi." - Helga Fogstad, Mkurugenzi Mtendaji, PMNCH "Kuzaliwa mapema kunaonyesha kwa nini lazima tuongeze uwekezaji na kuwajibika kwa kuzaliwa mapema – chanzo kikubwa zaidi cha kifo cha watoto walio chini ya umri wa miaka mitano."

translations.source: Progress is flatlining for maternal and newborn health, as well as the prevention of stillbirths, and is now pushed back further through the devastating combination of COVID-19, climate change, expanding conflicts and rising living costs. “By working together in partnership – governments, donors, the private sector, civil society, parents, and health professionals – we can sound the alarm about this “silent emergency”, and bring preterm prevention and care efforts to the forefront of national health and development efforts, building human capital by supporting families, societies and economies everywhere. ”Gabriela and Jerome Foster, Las Vegas, United States: parents of Jalen Foster, and founders of Jalen’s Gift Foundation “Our foundation was founded after the loss of our son, Jalen Foster, who was a preterm stillbirth at 27 weeks due to prenatal onset of a bacteria known as group B streptococcus. Losing our son was devastating, and there was no support in the community and that’s when we decided to provide a place of comfort and hope for families who have lost a baby.
translations.reference: Maendeleo yanadorora kwa afya ya uzazi na watoto wachanga, vilevile kuzuia watoto wanaokufa, na sasa yamerejeshwa nyuma zaidi kupitia mchanganyiko mbaya wa UVIKO_19, mabadiliko ya hewa, migogoro inayoongezeka na kupanda kwa gharama za maisha. "Kwa kufanya kazi pamoja kwa ushirikiano- serikali, wafadhili, sekta binafsi, asasi za kiraia, wazazi, na wataalamu wa afya - tunaweza kupinga kengele kuhusu "Dharura hii ya kimya", na kuleta jitihada za kuzuia na matunzo mbele ya afya ya kitaifa na juhudi za maendeleo, kujenga mtaji wa watu kwa kusaidia familia, jamii na uchumi kila mahali". "Gabriela na Jerome Foster, Las Vegaz, Marekani: wazazi wa Janel Foster, na waanzilishi wa Wakafu wa Jalen Gift. "Wakfu wetu ulianzishwa baada ya kufiwa na mtoto wetu wa kiume, Jalen Foster, ambae alijifungua mtoto Njiti akiwa na wiki 27 kutokana na kutokea kwa Bakteria wanaojulikana kama Streptococcus kundi B. Kumpoteza mtoto wetu wa kiume kulituumiza sana, na hakukuwa na usaidizi katika jamii na ndipo tulipoamua kutoa mahali pa faraja na matumaini kwa familia ambazo zimepoteza mtoto.

translations.source: UAE and WHO deliver air lift of critical medical supplies to Sudan The United Arab Emirates (UAE) and the World Health Organization have delivered 30 tons of urgent medical supplies to Sudan today. A plane carrying supplies for injury treatment, emergency surgeries, and essential drugs arrived in Port Sudan Airport early this morning. The shipment, valued at US$ 444 000, is the first that WHO has been able to deliver by air to Sudan since the outbreak of the conflict. WHO distributed supplies to heath facilities prior to the escalation of conflict.
translations.reference: UAE na WHO zinapeleka vifaa muhimu vya matibabu nchini sudani Umoja wa falme za kiarabu (UAE) na shirika la afya Duniani zimewasilisha tani 30 za vifaa vya dharura vya matibabu nchini sudani leo. Ndege iliyobeba vifaa vya matibabu ya majeraha, upasuaji wa dharura, dawa muhimu iliwasili katika uwanja wa ndege wa Port sudan mapema leo asubuhi. Shehena hiyo, yenye thamani ya Dola za kimarekani 444,000, ni ya kwanza ambayo WHO imeweza kuwasilisha kwa ndege hadi Sudani tangu kuzuka kwa mzozo huo. WHO ilisambaza vifaa kwa vituo vya afya kabla ya kuongezeka kwa mgogoro.

Jamhuri ya Muungano wa Falme za Kiarabu (UAE) na Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) wameleta tani 30 za vifaa vya matibabu dharura kwenda Sudan leo. Ndege iliyo beba vifaa vya matibabu ya majeraha, upasuaji wa dharura, na dawa muhimu ilifika Uwanja wa Ndege wa Port Sudan mapema asubuhi. Mto wa samani, ambao thamani yake ni Dola za Marekani 444,000, ni wa kwanza WHO imeweza kuleta kwa ndege kwenda Sudan tangu mlipuko wa mapigano. WHO ilisambaza vifaa kwa vituo vya afya kabla ya ongezeko la mapigano.

**Notes on the translation and Swahili.gs considerations:**

*   **Swahili.gs** aims for a standardized, widely understandable form of Swahili. I've tried to adhere to this.
*   **Loanwords:** I've used loanwords like "ndege" (plane) and "Uwanja wa Ndege" (airport) as they are common and accepted in modern Swahili.
*   **Capitalization:** Proper nouns (UAE, WHO, Sudan, Port Sudan) are capitalized.
*   **Numbers:** Numbers are written as numerals.
*   **Currency:**  "Dola za Marekani" is used for US dollars.
*   **Word Order:** I've followed standard Swahili sentence structure (Subject-Verb-Object where appropriate).

translations.source: These were exhausted after a few days given the number of injured. The dispatch of the aircraft comes as part of the UAE's continuous relief efforts in support of the Sudanese people and reflects its deep relations with Sudan, particularly in light of the current situation the country is facing. The relief is also an extension of the UAE’s humanitarian vision and underscores its relations with other countries based on human fraternity and its commitment to assisting others during crises and emergency situations. Dr Reem bint Ebrahim Al Hashimy, Minister of State for International Cooperation, said that the UAE "continues to work closely alongside the World Health Organization to ensure the successful deployment of the critical logistics operations to send urgent medical and food aid to help address the current crisis in Sudan. In parallel with calls for all sides of the current conflict to immediately cease hostilities and de-escalate a situation that is causing untold suffering for the Sudanese people, the UAE is steadfast in its commitment to providing aid and assistance to countries in times of need.
translations.reference: Viliishiwa baada ya siku chache kutokana idadi ya waliojeruhiwa. Kutumwa kwa ndege hiyo kunakuja kama sehemu ya juhudi za umoja wa falme za kiarabu kuwaunga mkono watu wa Sudani na kuakisi uhusiano wake wa kina na Sudani, hasa kwa kuzingatia hali ya sasa ya nchi hiyo. Pia misaada hiyo ni upanuzi wa maono ya kibinadamu ya UAE na inasisitiza uhusiano wake na nchi nyingie na kulingana na udugu wa kibinadamu na kujitolea kwake kusaidia wengine wakati wa majanga na hali za Dharura. Dk Reem binti Ebrahim Al hashimy, waziri wa nchi na ushirikiano wa kimataifa, alisema kuwa UAE "inaendelea kufanya kazi kwa karibu na shirika la afya Duniani ili kuhakikisha kupelekwa kwa shughuli muhimu za usafirishaji kutuma msaada wa haraka wa matibabu na chakula kusaisaidia kukabiliana na mzozo wa sasa nchini sudani. Sambamba na kutoa wito kwa pande zote za mzozo wa sasa kusitisha mara moja uhasama na kupunguza hali inayosababisha mateso yasiyoelezeka kwa watu wa Sudani, UAE iko imara katika ahadi yake ya kutoa misaada ya usaidizi kwa nchi wakati wa mahitaji.

translations.source: The UAE is particularly focused on providing for the most vulnerable groups affected by the situation in Sudan, especially the sick, children, the elderly, and women who are most at risk from the ongoing concerning conflict – and these relief flights will directly address the most pressing gaps in medical and food provision. The UAE's deeply held humanitarian values have meant that it has ensured it has continued to communicate to the world its robust and unrelenting dedication to strengthening peace, security and stability, not just regionally, but also worldwide, whilst alongside its partners and the international community, the country continues to assist the Sudanese people in times of crisis.” The health supplies include enough trauma, emergency surgical supplies, and essential medicines to immediately reach 165 000 people who are in desperate need of humanitarian aid. Health facilities across the country report they have run out of basic health supplies and that national medical stores are no longer accessible due to the security situation. Among other support, WHO is deploying two emergency logisticians travelling with the cargo to ensure that these supplies are immediately distributed to 13 major health facilities to support healthcare workers and extend care to those in need.
translations.reference: Umoja wa falme za kiarabu unalenga hasa kutoa mahitaji ya makundi yaliyo hatarini zaidi yaliyoathiriwa na hali nchini Sudani, hususani wagonjwa, watoto, wazee na wanawake ambao wapo katika hatari zaidi kutokana na mzozo unaoendelea - na safari hizi za ndege moja kwa moja zitatua mapengo makubwa zaidi katika utoaji wa matibabu na chakula. Maadili ya kibinadmu yaliyoshikiliwa kwa kina ya UAE yamemaanisha kwamba imehakikisha kuwa imeendelea kuwasiliana na ulimwengu kujitolea kwake kwa nguvu na kwa kudumu katika kuimarisha amani, usalama na utulivu, sio tu kikanda, lakini pia Duniani kote, wakati wa pamoja na washirika wake na jumuia ya kimataifa, nchi imendelea kusaidia watu wa Sudani wakati wa machafuko" vifaa ni pamoja na kiwewe cha kutosha, vifaa vya upasuaji wa Dharura, na dawa muhimu kufikia mara moja watu 165,000 ambao wanahitaji sana msaada wa kibinadamu. Vituo vya afya kote nchini vinaripoti kuwa vimekosa vifaa vya msingi vya afya na kwamba Bohari ya Taifa ya Dawa haifikii tena kutokana na hali ya usalama. Miongoni mwa msaada mwingine, WHO inapeleka wataalamu wawili wa vifaa vya Dharura wanaosafiri na mizigo ili kuhakikisha kwamba vifaa hivi vinasambazwa mara moja kwa vituo vikuu 13 vya afya ili kusaidia wahudumu wa afya na kupanua huduma kwa wale wanaohitaji.
translations.hypothesis:

**Swahili.gs Translation:**

"UAE inaangazia sana kutoa msaada kwa makundi yenye ulinzi mdogo zaidi yaliyoathirika na hali ya Sudan, hasa wagonjwa, watoto, wazee, na wanawake ambao wako hatarini zaidi kutokana na mapigano yanayoendelea na yanayo wasiwisha – na safari hizi za msaada zitashughulikia pengo kubwa zaidi katika utoaji wa dawa na chakula.  Mali ya ubinadamu iliyo mizizi mizito katika UAE imehakikisha kuwa imeendelea kuwasiliana na ulimwengu kuhusu kujitolea kwake thabiti na bila kusitishwa kuimarisha amani, usalama, na utulivu, si tu katika eneo, lakini pia duniani kote, wakati inasaidia watu wa Sudan katika nyakati za mgogoro pamoja na washirika wake na jumuiya ya kimataifa.  Usafirishaji wa vifaa vya afya unajumuisha dawa za matibabu, vifaa vya upasuaji wa dharura, na dawa muhimu ambazo zinaweza kuwafikia watu 165,000 mara moja, na wanao hitaji la msaada wa kibinadamu.  Vituo vya afya kote nchini vinaripoti kwamba wamepoteza vifaa vya msingi vya afya na kuwa ghala za dawa za kitaifa hazipatikani tena kutokana na hali ya usalama.  Kati ya msaada mwingine, WHO inatuma wataalamu wawili wa usafirishaji wa dharura wanaosafiri na mizigo ili kuhakikisha kuwa vifaa hivi vinasambazwa mara moja kwa vituo 13 vikuu vya afya ili kuunga mkono wataalamu wa afya na kupanua huduma kwa wale wanaohitaji."

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"UAE inaangazia sana..."** -  "Focuses particularly" is translated as "inaangazia sana" which means "highlights greatly" or "focuses strongly." It conveys the emphasis.
*   **"makundi yenye ulinzi mdogo zaidi"** - "Most vulnerable groups" is translated as "makundi yenye ulinzi mdogo zaidi."  "Ulinzi" means protection, and "mdogo zaidi" means smallest/least. This captures the idea of being most vulnerable.
*   **"yanayoendelea na yanayo wasiwisha"** - "Ongoing concerning conflict" is translated as "yanayoendelea na yanayo wasiwisha."  "Yanayoendelea" means ongoing, and "yanayo wasiwisha" means "causing worry/concern."
*   **"Mali ya ubinadamu iliyo mizizi mizito"** - "Deeply held humanitarian values" is translated as "Mali ya ubinadamu iliyo mizizi mizito." "Mali ya ubinadamu" means humanitarian values, and "iliyo mizizi mizito" means "deeply rooted."
*   **"kujitolea kwake thabiti na bila kusitishwa"** - "Robust and unrelenting dedication" is translated as "kujitolea kwake thabiti na bila kusitishwa." "Thabiti" means steadfast/stable, and "bila kusitishwa" means "without interruption/unrelenting."
*   **"nyakati za mgogoro"** - "Times of crisis" is translated as "nyakati za mgogoro."
*   **"wanao hitaji la msaada wa kibinadamu"** - "In desperate need of humanitarian aid" is translated as "wanao hitaji la msaada wa kibinadamu."
*   **"Vituo vya afya kote nchini"** - "Health facilities across the country" is translated as "Vituo vya afya kote nchini."
*   **"ghala za dawa za kitaifa hazipatikani tena"** - "National medical stores are no longer accessible" is translated as "ghala za dawa za kitaifa hazipatikani tena."
*   **"wataalamu wa usafirishaji wa dharura"** - "Emergency logisticians" is translated as "wataalamu wa usafirishaji wa dharura."
*   **"kuunga mkono wataalamu wa afya"** - "To support healthcare workers" is translated as "kuunga mkono wataalamu wa afya."

translations.source: WHO has another 30 metric tons of supplies for malaria and noncommunicable diseases like diabetes and hypertension, conditions that can become deadly if left untreated. These and about 23 000 blood bags are being readied within the WHO global logistics hub in the International Humanitarian City, and WHO is currently exploring all possibilities for delivering these supplies to Sudan as quickly as possible in collaboration with the Sudanese Ministry of Health. Dr Michael Ryan, Executive Director, of the WHO Health Emergencies Programme, said, “The WHO Global Logistics Hub in the UAE has become a centerpiece of rapid supply response to acute events globally. Supporting over 140 countries across all 6 WHO geographic regions, the hub provides a lifeline to Member States experiencing health emergencies arising from outbreaks of diseases, disasters such as floods and earthquakes, and the impact of conflict on health systems. The support provided by the UAE enables WHO to be at the forefront of these health emergency responses – delivering specialist medical aid to those in need wherever and whenever it is needed.
translations.reference: WHO ina tani nyingine 30 za vifaa kwa ajili ya Malaria na magonjwa yasiyoambukiza kama vile Kisukari na Shikinizo la damu, hali ambayo inaweza kuwa mbaya kama haitibiwi. Mifumo hii na takribani 23,000 ya mifuko ya damu inasomwa ndani ya kituo cha vifaa cha kimataifa cha WHO katika jiji la kimataifa la kibinadamu, na WHO kwa sasa inachunguza uwezekano wote wa kuwasilisha vifaa hivi nchini Sudani haraka iwezekanavyo kwa ushirikiano na wizara ya afya ya Sudani. Dk Michael Ryan, mkurugenzi mtendaji, wa mpango wa Dharura ya afya wa WHO, alisema, "kituo cha usafirishaji cha WHO huko UAE kimekuwa kitovu cha muitiko wa haraka wa matukio ya Dharura ulimwenguni. Husaidia zaidi ya nchi 140 katika maeneo yote 6 ya kijografia ya WHO, kitovu hutoa njia ya kuokoa maisha kwa nchi wanachama zinazokabiliwa na Dharura za kiafya zinazotokana na milipuko ya magonjwa, majanga kama vile mafuriko na matetemeko ya ardhi, na athari za migogoro kwenye mifumo ya afya. Msaada unaotolewa na UAE huwezesha WHO kuwa mstari wa mbele katika majibu haya ya Dharura ya afya- kutoa msaada wa matibabu wa kibingwa kwa wale wanaohitaji popote na wakati wowote inapohitajika.

Hii na mifuko ya damu takriban 23,000 zinaandaliwa katika kitovu cha usafirishaji wa kimataifa cha WHO katika Jiji la Kimataifa la Humanitarian, na WHO inatafuta kila uwezekano wa kutoa usafirishaji huu kwenda Sudan haraka iwezekanavyo kwa kushirikiana na Wizara ya Afya ya Sudan. translations.source: As part of efforts to strengthen the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience the UAE, WHO, and partners are working hand-in-hand to deliver supplies to reach the world’s most vulnerable in their time of greatest need.” For his part, Dr Ahmed Al-Mandhari, WHO Regional Director for the Eastern Mediterranean, said, “The World Health Organization continues to coordinate with the Sudanese health authorities to deliver essential health supplies through all available pathways. Due to the conflict, hospitals, pharmacies and primary health care facilities have stocked out of essential, life-saving medicines. The arrival of an additional 30 metric tons of trauma and emergency surgery supplies as well as essential medicines will enable WHO to support 13 major health facilities and re-establish health care services for those in need. Our global logistics hub in Dubai plays a vital role in ensuring an uninterrupted supply of health commodities and we are grateful for the support provided by the United Arab Emirates to facilitate our first charter flight of these desperately needed supplies.
translations.reference: Kama sehemu ya juhudi za kuimarisha usanifu wa kimataifa kwa ajili ya maandalizi ya Dharura ya kiafya, muitikio, na ustahimilivu UAE, WHO, na washirika wanafanya kazi bega kwa bega kupeana vifaa ili kuwafikia waliohatarini zaidi Duniani katika wakati wao wa uhitaji mkubwa" Kwa upande wake, Dk Ahmed Al- Mandhari, mkurugenzi wa WHO kanda ya mashariki ya Mediterania, alisema, "shirika la afya Duniani linaendelea kuratibu na mamlaka ya afya ya Sudani kuwasilisha vifaa muhimu vya afya kupitia njia zote zinazopatikana. Kwa sababu ya mzozo, hospitali, maduka ya dawa na vituo vya huduma ya afya ya msingi vimekosa dawa muhimu, Dawa za kuokoa maisha. Kuwasili kwa tani 30 za ziada za vifaa vya kiwewe na upasuaji wa Dharura vilevile dawa muhimu kutawezesha WHO kusaidia vituo vikuu 13 vya afya na kuanzisha upya huduma za afya kwa wale wanaohitajika. Kituo chetu cha usafirishaji cha kimataifa huko Dubai kina jukumu muhimu katika kuhakikisha usambazaji usiokatizwa wa bidhaa za afya na tunashukuru kwa usaidizi unaotolewa na umoja wa falme za kiarabu kuwezesha safari yetu ya kwanza ya kukodi ya vifaa hivi vinavyohitajika sana.

translations.source: It is support such as this that shows in action our Regional vision of health for all, by all.” The medical aid provided by the World Health Organization through the UAE accounted for approximately 55% of the total medical supplies provided to Sudan from abroad in 2022, reflecting the UAE's position as a major hub for international humanitarian assistance.

translations.source: Collaborating locally is key for progress globally towards health-related SDGs A new progress report is being jointly released today by the 13 signatory agencies of the Global Action Plan for Healthy Lives and Well-being for All (SDG3 GAP). The report entitled “What worked? What didn’t? What’s next?” reflects four years of joint work by major multilateral agencies outlining what has and has not worked in strengthening collaboration and support to accelerate country progress towards health-related Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). This year marks the halfway point toward the SDGs, but the world is lagging behind to reach the global goals. Since its launch in 2019, the SDG3 GAP enabled creation of new collaboration structures between the signatory agencies in key areas such as sustainable financing and primary health care among others.
translations.reference: Kushirikiana ndani ya nchi ni muhimu kwa maendeleo ya kimataifa kuelekea SDGs zinazohusiana na afya Ripoti mpya ya maendeleo inatolewa kwa pamoja leo na mashirika 13 yaliyotia saini mpango wa utekelezaji wa kimataifa wa maisha ya afya na ustawi kwa wote (SDG3 GAP). Ripoti yenye anuani "Ni nini kilifanya kazi? Nini haikufanya? Nini kinafuata?" inaakisi miaka minne ya kazi ya pamoja ya mashirika makubwa ya kimataifa inayoelezea kile ambacho hakijafanya kazi katika kuimarisha ushirikiano na usaidizi ili kuharakisha maendeleo ya nchi kuelekea malengo ya maendeleo endelevu (SDGs) yanayohusiana na afya. Mwaka huu ni ishara ya nusu ya kuelekea SDGs, lakini Dunia ipo nyuma kufikia malengo ya kimataifa. Tangu kuzinduliwa kwake mwaka 2019, SDG3 GAP iliwezesha kuundwa kwa miundo mipya ya ushirikiano kati ya mashirika yaliyotia saini katika maeneo muhimu kama vile ufadhili endelevu na huduma ya afya ya msingi miongoni mwa mengine.

translations.source: At least 67 countries engaged in on one or more of the SDG3 GAP accelerator themes. "We have made important progress, but we still have a long path to travel to improve the way that multilateral organizations work together to support countries. We must listen to what countries tell us and act upon their guidance,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General and Chair of the SDG3 GAP Principals Group. “I thank the partners for their collaboration and for the honest self-assessments contained in this report. "The report provides six key recommendations to ensure that countries benefit from a more streamlined support and to realize the commitments made in the SDG3 GAP.
translations.reference: Angalau nchi 67 zimejihusisha kwenye moja au zaidi ya maudhui ya kichochezi ya SDG3 GAP. "Tumipiga hatua muhimu, lakini bado tuna njia ndefu ya kusafiri ili kuboresha jinsi mashirika ya kimataifa yanayofanya kazi pamoja kusaidia nchi. Lazima tusikilize kile ambacho nchi zinatuambia na kuchukua hatua kulingana na muongozo wao", alisema Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO na mwenyekiti wa kundi la wakuu wa SDG3 GAP. "Nawashukuru washirika kwa ushirikiano wao na kwa kujitathmini kwa uaminifu uliomo katika ripoti hii. "Ripoti inatoa mapendekezo sita muhimu ili kuhakikisha kuwa nchi zinanufaika kutokana na usaidizi ulioboreshwa zaidi na kutimiza ahadi zilizotolewa katika SDG3 GAP.
translations.hypothesis: Angalau nchi 67 zilijihusisha na moja au zaidi ya mada za kasi za SDG3 GAP. "Tumefanya maendeleo muhimu, lakini bado tuna njia ndefu ya kusafiri ili kuboresha jinsi mashirika mengi yanavyofanya kazi pamoja kusaidia nchi. Lazima tusikilize kile nchi zetu wanatuambia na kutenda kulingana na mwongozo wao," alisema Dkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa WHO na Rais wa Kikundi cha Wakuu wa SDG3 GAP. "Ninawashukuru washirika kwa ushirikiano wao na kwa tathmini za kujitegemea zilizomo katika ripoti hii. Ripoti hii hutoa mapendekezo sita muhimu ili kuhakikisha kuwa nchi zinanufaika na msaada uliorahisishwa na kutimiza ahadi zilizofanywa katika SDG3 GAP."

translations.source: These include: continue the SDG3 GAP improvement cycle for health by seeking the views of Member States on how we collaborate at country-level and responding to related recommendations; maintain GAP as a platform for collaboration; foster stronger collaboration at the country level on primary health care and explore new thematic topics such as climate resilient health systems; jointly apply new approaches at country level such as the delivery for impact approach;
translations.reference: Mapendekezo hayo ni pamoja na Kuendeleza mzunguko wa uboreshaji wa SDG3 GAP kwa afya kwa kutafuta maoni ya nchi wanachama kuhusu jinsi tunavyoshirikiana katika ngazi ya nchi na kujibu mapendekezo yanayohusiana; Kudumisha GAP kama jukwaa la ushirikiano; Kukuza ushirikiano wenye nguvu katika ngazi ya chini kuhusu huduma ya afya ya msingi na kuchunguza mada mpya kama vile mifumo ya afya inayostahimili hali ya hewa. Kutumia kwa pamoja mbinu mpya katika ngazi ya nchi kama vile uwasilishaji kwa mbinu ya athari;

translations.source: engage more with civil society; and work with Member States to strengthen incentives for collaboration through political leadership, governance direction and funding to support collaboration. Key findings of the report will feed into discussions of the next United Nations General Assembly SDG Summit and the High-level Meeting on Universal Health Coverage in September 2023. The months leading up to these key events provide an opportunity for a joint push by Member States and multilateral agencies to identify ways on how to implement the recommendations and to prepare the ground to fully leverage stronger collaboration and accelerate progress towards the health-related SDGs in the second half of the SDG timeframe. Note to the editor:

translations.source: The SDG3 GAP describes how the 13 signatory agencies will adopt new ways of working, building on existing successful collaborations, and jointly align their support around national plans and strategies that are country-owned and -led. The signatories to the SDG3 GAP are Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance; Global Financing Facility for Women, Children and Adolescents (GFF); Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria (Global Fund); International Labour Organization (ILO);
translations.reference: SDG3 GAP inaeleza jinsi mashirika 13 yaliyotia saini yatatumia njia mpya za kufanya kazi, kuendeleza ushirikiano uliopo kwenye mafanikio, na kuoanisha kwa pamoja msaada wao katika mipango na mikakati ya kitaifa ambayo inamilikiwa na kuongozwa na nchi. Waliotia saini SDG3 GAP ni Gavi, Muungano wa Chanjo; Ufadhili wa kituo cha kimataifa kwa wanawake, watoto na vijana (GFF); Mfuko wa kimataifa wa kupambana na UKIMWI, kifua kikuu na Malaria (mfuko wa kimataifa); Shirika la kazi la kimataifa (ILO);

translations.source: New survey results show health systems starting to recover from pandemic After three years of the COVID-19 pandemic, health systems in countries have started showing the first major signs of health system recovery, according to the WHO interim report on the “Fourth round of the global pulse survey on continuity of essential health services during the COVID-19 pandemic: November 2022–January 2023”. By early 2023, countries reported experiencing reduced disruptions in the delivery of routine health services, but highlighted the need to invest in recovery and stronger resilience for the future. Among the 139 countries that responded to the fourth round of WHO’s pulse survey, countries reported continued disruptions in almost one-quarter of services on average. In 84 countries where trend analysis is possible, the percentage of disrupted services declined on average from 56% in July-September 2020 to 23% in November 2022- January 2023.
translations.reference: Matokeo mapya ya uchunguzi yanaonyesha mifano ya afya ikianza kurejeshwa kutokana na janga Baada ya miaka mitatu ya janga la UVIKO-19, mifumo ya afya katika nchi imeanza kuonyesha dalili kuu za kwanza za kuimarika kwa mfumo wa afya kulingana na ripoti ya muda ya WHO juu ya "Duru ya nne ya uchunguzi wa mapigo ya kimataifa juu ya kuendelea kwa huduma muhimu za afya wakati wa janga la UVIKO-19: Novemba 2022 Januari 2023". Ifikapo mapema mwaka 2023, nchi ziliripoti kukabiliwa na usumbufu mdogo katika utoaji wa huduma za afya za kawaida, lakini zilionyesha haja ya kuwekeza katika kurejeshwa na kustahimili nguvu zaidi kwa siku zijazo. Kati ya nchi 139 zilizojibu duru ya nne ya uchunguzi wa mapigo ya moyo wa WHO, nchi ziliripoti kuendelea kukatika kwa karibu robo moja ya huduma kwa wastani. Katika nchi 84 ambapo uchunguzi wa mwenendo unawezekana, asilimia ya huduma zilizokatizwa ilipungua kwa wstani kutoka 56% mwezi Julai - Septemba 2020 hadi 23% mnamo Novemba 2022 - Januari 2023.
